#************************************************************************
# Configuration file for the testnap utility
#
#
# You use testnap to manually interact with the database and to test your
# custom applications.
#
# This configuration file is automatically installed and configured with
# default values during Portal installation. You can edit this file to:
#   -- change the default values of the entries.
#   -- disable an entry by inserting a crosshatch (#) at the start of
#        the line.
#   -- enable a commented entry by removing the crosshatch (#).
# 
# Before you make any changes to this file, save a backup copy.
#
# When editing this file, follow the instructions in each section.
# For more information on the general syntax of configuration entries,
# see "Reference Guide to Portal Configuration Files" in the Portal
# online documentation.
#************************************************************************


#======================================================================
#
# You can edit this file to suit your specific configuration:
#  -- You can change the default values of an entry.
#  -- You can exclude an optional entry by adding the # symbol
#     at the beginning of the line.
#  -- You can include a commented entry by removing the # symbol.
#
# Before you make any changes to this file, save a backup copy.
#
# To edit this file, follow the instructions in the commented sections.
# For more information on the general syntax of configuration entries,
# see "Reference Guide to Portal Configuration Files" in the Portal
# online documentation.
#======================================================================

#=======================================================================
# ptr_virtual_time
# 
# Enables the use of pin_virtual_time to advance Portal time, and 
# specifies the shared file for use by all Portal mangers.
#
# The entry has 2 values
#
# #/Empty     	to disable / enable pin_virtual_time in all pin.conf files
#		default = #	(disable)
#
# <filepath> 	location and name of the pin_virtual_time file shared by all Portal managers.
#		default = ${PIN_HOME}/lib/pin_virtual_time_file
#
#=======================================================================
- - pin_virtual_time ${PIN_HOME}/lib/pin_virtual_time_file



#========================================================================
# cm_ptr
# 
# Specifies a pointer to the CM or CMMP. 
#
# Use a separate entry for each CM or CMMP. If testnap can't find the
# first CM or CMMP, it looks for the next in the list.
#
# Each entry includes three values:
# 
#     <protocol> = "ip", for this release
#     <host>     = the name or IP address of the computer running the
#                    CM or CMMP
#     <port>     = the TCP port number of the CM or CMMP on this computer
#
# The port number should match a corresponding cm_ports entry with
# the same port number in the CM or CMMP configuration file. The 
# default, 11960, is a commonly specified port for the CM or CMMP.
#========================================================================
- nap cm_ptr ip congero-brm-training 11960


#========================================================================
# logfile
#
# Specifies the path to the log file for the sample application
#========================================================================
- sample_act    logfile sample_act.pinlog
- sample_del    logfile sample_del.pinlog
- sample_app    logfile sample_app.pinlog 
- sample_search logfile sample_search.pinlog 
- sample_who    logfile sample_who.pinlog


#========================================================================
# login_type
#
# Specifies whether the login name and password are required.
#
# The value for this entry can be:
#
#    0 = Only a user ID is required.
#    1 = (Default) Both a name and a password are required.
#
# By default, CMs require a user login and password when requesting an open
# context using PCM_CONTEXT_OPEN. However, you can remove this authentication
# requirement by configuring the CM with a cm_login_module of
# cm_login_null.so
#========================================================================
- nap login_type 1


#========================================================================
# login_name
#
# Specifies the login name to use when testnap connects to the CM. 
#========================================================================
- nap login_name root.0.0.0.1


#========================================================================
# login_pw
#
# Specifies the password to use when testnap connects to the CM.
#========================================================================
#- nap login_pw   


#========================================================================
# userid
#
# Specifies the database number and service type for the Portal
# database. 
#
# The CM uses the database number to identify the Portal database 
# and to connect to the correct Data Manager. For connections that don't
# require a login name and password, the CM also passes the service
# type to the database.
#
# The database number, in the form 0.0.0.N, is the number assigned to 
# your Portal database when you installed the system. The default
# is 0.0.0.1.
#
# The service type is /service/pcm_client and the ID is set based on login_name.
# DO NOT change these values.
#========================================================================
- - userid 0.0.0.1 /service/pcm_client 1


#========================================================================
# enable_ssl
#
# Specifies whether TLS will be enabled for PCM connections
#
#    0 - TLS is disabled.
#    1 - TLS is enabled.
#
#========================================================================
- nap enable_ssl 0

#=======================================
#  Field PIN_FLD_SESSION_USAGE_GROUP_1
#=======================================

SUBSTRUCT PIN_FLD_SESSION_USAGE_GROUP_1 {

	ID = 10004;
}


#=======================================
#  Field PIN_FLD_TRANSACTION_ID
#=======================================

DECIMAL PIN_FLD_TRANSACTION_ID {

	ID = 8102;
}


#=======================================
#  Field PIN_FLD_INPUT_TOKENS
#=======================================

INT PIN_FLD_INPUT_TOKENS {

	ID = 10005;
}


#=======================================
#  Field PIN_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS
#=======================================

INT PIN_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS {

	ID = 10006;
}


#=======================================
#  Field PIN_FLD_PROMPT_TEXT
#=======================================

STRING PIN_FLD_PROMPT_TEXT {

	ID = 10007;
}

#  Field PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE

	ID = 10008;

#=======================================
#  Storable Class /service/nextaig3
#=======================================

STORABLE CLASS /service/nextaig3 {

	READ_ACCESS = "Self";
	WRITE_ACCESS = "Self";
	IS_PARTITIONED = "0";
	EVENT_TYPE = "NONE";

	#===================
	#  Fields 
	#===================

	ARRAY PIN_FLD_ARGS {

		DESCR = "Terminal Server Protocol Extensions Array. List of (name, value) pairs which are used to set the IP session characteristics. Element id not significant.  Multiple entries with same name may be used if appropriate. See PCM_OP_TERM_POL_AUTHORIZE.";
		ORDER = 0;
		AUDITABLE = 0;
		ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
		SERIALIZABLE = 0;

		#===================
		#  Fields 
		#===================

		STRING PIN_FLD_NAME {

			DESCR = "Extension name. For RADIUS, this name must match a name from the dictionary. e.g. Framed-IP-Netmask, Framed-MTU.";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 255;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_VALUE {

			DESCR = "Extension value. Stringified version of the value for the corresponding extension name. e.g. for IP addrs this is in dotted decimal notation. For enumerated values, either enumerated name or decimal value may be used (e.g. PPP or 1 for Framed-Protocol)";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 1023;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

	}

}

#  Storable Class /service/nextaig3

	#===================
	#  Fields 
	#===================

	ARRAY PIN_FLD_ARGS {

		SQL_TABLE = "service_nextaig3_args_t";
		SQL_STORAGE = "tablespace pin00 storage (initial 200k next 200k maxextents unlimited pctincrease 0 ) ";

		#===================
		#  Fields 
		#===================

		STRING PIN_FLD_NAME {

			SQL_COLUMN = "name";
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_VALUE {

			SQL_COLUMN = "value";
		}

	}

}


#=======================================
#  Storable Class /event/session/usageg1
#=======================================

STORABLE CLASS /event/session/usageg1 {

	LABEL = "NextAI Session Usage Group 1";
	READ_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";
	WRITE_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";
	DESCR = "Session usage event Group 1 for NextAI. Rated via PCM_OP_ACT_LOAD_SESSION. Linked to /service/nextaig1. Stores transaction ID, tokens, prompt text, model code (3.0/3.5).";
	IS_PARTITIONED = "1";
	PARTITION_MODE = "Finite";
	EVENT_TYPE = "USAGE_PREPAID";

	#===================
	#  Fields 
	#===================

	ARRAY PIN_FLD_SESSION_USAGE_GROUP_1 {

		LABEL = "NextAI Session Usage Group 1";
		DESCR = "Array holding NextAI session usage data for Group 1 on /event/session/usageg1.";
		ORDER = 0;
		AUDITABLE = 0;
		ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
		SERIALIZABLE = 0;

		#===================
		#  Fields 
		#===================

		INT PIN_FLD_INPUT_TOKENS_GROUP_1 {

			LABEL = "Input Tokens";
			DESCR = "Count of input tokens for the AI session (Group 1).\n";
			ORDER = 0;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE {

			LABEL = "Model Code";
			DESCR = "AI model for rating (Group 1): 3.0 or 3.5.";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 8;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		INT PIN_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS {

			LABEL = "Output Tokens";
			DESCR = "Count of output tokens for the AI session (Group 1).";
			ORDER = 0;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_PROMPT_TEXT {

			LABEL = "Prompt Text";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 255;
			CREATE = Optional;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		DECIMAL PIN_FLD_TRANSACTION_ID {

			LABEL = "Transaction ID";
			DESCR = "Unique ID for the NextAI session transaction (Group 1).\n";
			ORDER = 0;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

	}

}

#  Storable Class /event/session/usageg1

STORABLE CLASS /event/session/usageg1 IMPLEMENTATION ORACLE7 {


	#===================
	#  Fields 
	#===================

	ARRAY PIN_FLD_SESSION_USAGE_GROUP_1 {

		SQL_TABLE = "event_session_usageg1_t";
		SQL_STORAGE = "tablespace pin00 initrans 2 storage (initial 500k next 500k maxextents unlimited pctincrease 0 freelists 2 ) partition by range (obj_id0) (partition partition_historic values less than (35184372088832), partition partition_last values less than (MAXVALUE))";

		#===================
		#  Fields 
		#===================

		INT PIN_FLD_INPUT_TOKENS_GROUP_1 {

			SQL_COLUMN = "input_tokens";
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE {

			SQL_COLUMN = "model_code";
		}

		INT PIN_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS {

			SQL_COLUMN = "output_tokens";
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_PROMPT_TEXT {

			SQL_COLUMN = "prompt_text";
		}

		DECIMAL PIN_FLD_TRANSACTION_ID {

			SQL_COLUMN = "transaction_id";
		}

	}

}

	WRITE_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";

		ORDER = 0;
		AUDITABLE = 0;
		ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
		SERIALIZABLE = 0;

			DESCR = "Extension name. For RADIUS, this name must match a name from the dictionary. e.g. Framed-IP-Netmask, Framed-MTU.";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 255;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_VALUE {

			DESCR = "Extension value. Stringified version of the value for the corresponding extension name. e.g. for IP addrs this is in dotted decimal notation. For enumerated values, either enumerated name or decimal value may be used (e.g. PPP or 1 for Framed-Protocol)";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 1023;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

	}

}

<ConfigObject>
	<POID>0.0.0.1 /config/business_params 8590 0</POID>
	<ACCOUNT_OBJ>0.0.0.1 /account 1 0</ACCOUNT_OBJ>
	<DESCR>Business logic parameters for Rating</DESCR>
	<HOSTNAME>-</HOSTNAME>
	<IDENTIFICATION_CODE></IDENTIFICATION_CODE>
	<NAME>rating</NAME>
	<PROGRAM_NAME>-</PROGRAM_NAME>
	<PARAMS elem="0">
		<DESCR>This parameter decides if the system-wide credit limit override feature is enabled. When enabled by setting to 1, credit limit check is avoided irrespective of the credit limit override value set in the product. Default value is 0 which disables this feature.</DESCR>
		<PARAM_NAME>override_credit_limit</PARAM_NAME>
		<PARAM_TYPE>1</PARAM_TYPE>
		<PARAM_VALUE>0</PARAM_VALUE>
	</PARAMS>
	<PARAMS elem="1">
		<DESCR>This parameter decides if the tailormade products need to be maintained in the cache of C Rating Engine. When enabled by setting to 1, tailormade products are stored in cache. Default value is 0 which disables this feature</DESCR>
		<PARAM_NAME>enable_tailormade_cache</PARAM_NAME>
		<PARAM_TYPE>1</PARAM_TYPE>
		<PARAM_VALUE>0</PARAM_VALUE>
	</PARAMS>
	<PARAMS elem="2">
		<DESCR>This parameter enables or disables Global charge sharing.This is enabled by setting to 1.Default value is 0 which disables this feature.</DESCR>
		<PARAM_NAME>enable_global_charge_sharing</PARAM_NAME>
		<PARAM_TYPE>1</PARAM_TYPE>
		<PARAM_VALUE>0</PARAM_VALUE>
	</PARAMS>
	<PARAMS elem="3">
		<DESCR>This paramater indicates the maximum number of products or discounts that will be cached in C Rating Engine cache. Once the specified value is reached for either products/discounts then 10% of the products or discounts that are least used will be flushed from the cache. This is applicable only if this parameter is non-zero. Default value is 0 which disables this feature.</DESCR>
		<PARAM_NAME>products_discounts_threshold</PARAM_NAME>
		<PARAM_TYPE>1</PARAM_TYPE>
		<PARAM_VALUE>0</PARAM_VALUE>
	</PARAMS>
	<PARAMS elem="4">
		<DESCR>This parameter enables or disables rounding of purchase grant valid from validity to midnight.This works along with timestamp_rounding pin.conf configuration.This is enabled by setting to 1. Default value is 1 which enables this feature. If this flag is disabled and timestamp rounding is enabled , then the purchase grant will not be midnight rounded.Higher precedence of flag is given to the new business param.If the new flag is enabled and midnight rounding is enabled , the purchase fee grant will be midnight rounded.</DESCR>
		<PARAM_NAME>timestamp_rounding_for_purchase_grant</PARAM_NAME>
		<PARAM_TYPE>1</PARAM_TYPE>
		<PARAM_VALUE>1</PARAM_VALUE>
	</PARAMS>
	<PARAMS elem="5">
		<DESCR>This parameter enables or disables override amount in product currency. Default value is 0 which disables this feature. If this flag is disabled, then the override amount for a product will be in account currency.</DESCR>
		<PARAM_NAME>override_in_product_currency</PARAM_NAME>
		<PARAM_TYPE>1</PARAM_TYPE>
		<PARAM_VALUE>0</PARAM_VALUE>
	</PARAMS>
	<PARAMS elem="6">
		<DESCR>This parameter enables or disables caching of system/user products/discounts during cm startup. Bit fields of the integer value are used to specify the configuration options. Bit 0: precaches system products. Bit 1: precaches user products. Bit 2: precaches system discounts. Bit 3: precaches user discounts. Default value is 0. Any combination of bits can be set based on the precache requirement.</DESCR>
		<PARAM_NAME>rate_pre_cache_product_and_disc</PARAM_NAME>
		<PARAM_TYPE>1</PARAM_TYPE>
		<PARAM_VALUE>0</PARAM_VALUE>
	</PARAMS>
	<PARAMS elem="7">
		<DESCR>Default value is enable. If disabled then rating will skip pipeline call so that discounts are not processed if the product scale is 0.</DESCR>
		<PARAM_NAME>apply_discount_on_zero_charge</PARAM_NAME>
		<PARAM_TYPE>1</PARAM_TYPE>
		<PARAM_VALUE>1</PARAM_VALUE>
	</PARAMS>
	<PARAMS elem="8">
		<DESCR>This parameter decides if the credit limit of member account to be set zero or not in case of roll up. When enabled, credit limit of member account will be zero. Default value is enabled.</DESCR>
		<PARAM_NAME>reset_member_credit_limit</PARAM_NAME>
		<PARAM_TYPE>1</PARAM_TYPE>
		<PARAM_VALUE>1</PARAM_VALUE>
	</PARAMS>
	<PARAMS elem="9">
		<DESCR>Parameter to control whether the sharing groups feature is enabled. The default value is enabled (1). If disabled (0), the sharing groups feature will not be allowed.</DESCR>
		<PARAM_NAME>sharing_groups_enabled</PARAM_NAME>
		<PARAM_TYPE>1</PARAM_TYPE>

#=======================================
#  Storable Class /service/my_ai
#=======================================

STORABLE CLASS /service/my_ai {

	READ_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";
	WRITE_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";
	DESCR = "Provides dialup Internet access. Includes connection information, such as IP address and connection protocol.";
	IS_PARTITIONED = "0";
	EVENT_TYPE = "NONE";

	#===================
	#  Fields 
	#===================

	ARRAY PIN_FLD_ARGS {

		DESCR = "Terminal Server Protocol Extensions Array. List of (name, value) pairs which are used to set the IP session characteristics. Element id not significant.  Multmy_aile entries with same name may be used if appropriate. See PCM_OP_TERM_POL_AUTHORIZE.";
		ORDER = 0;
		AUDITABLE = 0;
		ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
		SERIALIZABLE = 0;

		#===================
		#  Fields 
		#===================

		STRING PIN_FLD_NAME {

			DESCR = "Extension name. For RADIUS, this name must match a name from the dictionary. e.g. Framed-IP-Netmask, Framed-MTU.";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 255;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_VALUE {

			DESCR = "Extension value. Stringified version of the value for the corresponding extension name. e.g. for IP addrs this is in dotted decimal notation. For enumerated values, either enumerated name or decimal value may be used (e.g. PPP or 1 for Framed-Protocol)";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 1023;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

	}

}

			SQL_COLUMN = "name";

		}

	}

		ORDER = 0;
		AUDITABLE = 0;
		ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
		SERIALIZABLE = 0;

		#  Fields 
		#===================

			DESCR = "input tokens";
			ORDER = 0;

			AUDITABLE = 0;

			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE_G1 {

			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 60;
			CREATE = Optional;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		INT PIN_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS_G1 {

			ORDER = 0;
			CREATE = Optional;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_PROMPT_TEXT_G1 {

			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 255;
			CREATE = Optional;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_TRANS_ID {

			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 60;
			CREATE = Optional;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

	}

}

#************************************************************************
# Configuration file for the testnap utility
#
#
# You use testnap to manually interact with the database and to test your
# custom applications.
#
# This configuration file is automatically installed and configured with
# default values during Portal installation. You can edit this file to:
#   -- change the default values of the entries.
#   -- disable an entry by inserting a crosshatch (#) at the start of
#        the line.
#   -- enable a commented entry by removing the crosshatch (#).
# 
# Before you make any changes to this file, save a backup copy.
#
# When editing this file, follow the instructions in each section.
# For more information on the general syntax of configuration entries,
# see "Reference Guide to Portal Configuration Files" in the Portal
# online documentation.
#************************************************************************


#======================================================================
#
# You can edit this file to suit your specific configuration:
#  -- You can change the default values of an entry.
#  -- You can exclude an optional entry by adding the # symbol
#     at the beginning of the line.
#  -- You can include a commented entry by removing the # symbol.
#
# Before you make any changes to this file, save a backup copy.
#
# To edit this file, follow the instructions in the commented sections.
# For more information on the general syntax of configuration entries,
# see "Reference Guide to Portal Configuration Files" in the Portal
# online documentation.
#======================================================================

#=======================================================================
# ptr_virtual_time
# 
# Enables the use of pin_virtual_time to advance Portal time, and 
# specifies the shared file for use by all Portal mangers.
#
# The entry has 2 values
#
# #/Empty     	to disable / enable pin_virtual_time in all pin.conf files
#		default = #	(disable)
#
# <filepath> 	location and name of the pin_virtual_time file shared by all Portal managers.
#		default = ${PIN_HOME}/lib/pin_virtual_time_file
#
#=======================================================================
- - pin_virtual_time ${PIN_HOME}/lib/pin_virtual_time_file



#========================================================================
# cm_ptr
# 
# Specifies a pointer to the CM or CMMP. 
#
# Use a separate entry for each CM or CMMP. If testnap can't find the
# first CM or CMMP, it looks for the next in the list.
#
# Each entry includes three values:
# 
#     <protocol> = "ip", for this release
#     <host>     = the name or IP address of the computer running the
#                    CM or CMMP
#     <port>     = the TCP port number of the CM or CMMP on this computer
#
# The port number should match a corresponding cm_ports entry with
# the same port number in the CM or CMMP configuration file. The 
# default, 11960, is a commonly specified port for the CM or CMMP.
#========================================================================
- nap cm_ptr ip congero-brm-training 11960


#========================================================================
# logfile
#
# Specifies the path to the log file for the sample application
#========================================================================
- sample_act    logfile sample_act.pinlog
- sample_del    logfile sample_del.pinlog
- sample_app    logfile sample_app.pinlog 
- sample_search logfile sample_search.pinlog 
- sample_who    logfile sample_who.pinlog


#========================================================================
# login_type
#
# Specifies whether the login name and password are required.
#
# The value for this entry can be:
#
#    0 = Only a user ID is required.
#    1 = (Default) Both a name and a password are required.
#
# By default, CMs require a user login and password when requesting an open
# context using PCM_CONTEXT_OPEN. However, you can remove this authentication
# requirement by configuring the CM with a cm_login_module of
# cm_login_null.so
#========================================================================
- nap login_type 1


#========================================================================
# login_name
#
# Specifies the login name to use when testnap connects to the CM. 
#========================================================================
- nap login_name root.0.0.0.1


#========================================================================
# login_pw
#
# Specifies the password to use when testnap connects to the CM.
#========================================================================
#- nap login_pw   


#========================================================================
# userid
#
# Specifies the database number and service type for the Portal
# database. 
#
# The CM uses the database number to identify the Portal database 
# and to connect to the correct Data Manager. For connections that don't
# require a login name and password, the CM also passes the service
# type to the database.
#
# The database number, in the form 0.0.0.N, is the number assigned to 
# your Portal database when you installed the system. The default
# is 0.0.0.1.
#
# The service type is /service/pcm_client and the ID is set based on login_name.
# DO NOT change these values.
#========================================================================
- - userid 0.0.0.1 /service/pcm_client 1


#========================================================================
# enable_ssl
#
# Specifies whether TLS will be enabled for PCM connections
#
#    0 - TLS is disabled.
#    1 - TLS is enabled.
#
#========================================================================
- nap enable_ssl 0

#========================================================================
# Custom AI Extension Fields
#
# Specifies an external data dictionary file that defines custom fields
# used by NextAI Service and Event groups. This entry allows the system
# data dictionary memory cache to map custom programmatic field names to
# their numeric database field IDs during initialization.
#
# This entry is required to prevent "Bad quantity expression" or "Unknown
# field" errors when parsing custom metrics in utilities like testnap or
# load_pin_rum.
#
# For changes to take effect, the CM must be restarted.
#
# Syntax
#
#    - - ops_fields_extension_file [absolute path to file]
#
# Parameters
#
#    [absolute path to file]  The complete directory path and filename
#                             of the custom .dat file containing your
#                             field mappings.
#
#========================================================================
- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/sys/test/nextaig1/customFieldsVisibility/nextaig1_fields.dat

/*
 * Input for parse_custom_ops_fields ŌĆö registers opcodes/fields in testnap client.
 * Run register_client_ext.sh on the VM, then add ops_fields_extension_file to
 * $PIN_HOME/sys/test/pin.conf (not CM pin.conf).
 */
#ifndef NAI_G1_PCM_EXT_H
#define NAI_G1_PCM_EXT_H

/* Opcodes (90001-90003) ŌĆö symbolic names for xop in .nap scripts */
#define NAI_OP_COMMIT_CUSTOMER           90001
#define NAI_OP_ACT_RATE                  90002
#define NAI_OP_ACT_GET_AVAILABLE_RES       90003

#define NAI_G1_OP_COMMIT_CUSTOMER        90001
#define NAI_G1_OP_ACT_RATE               90002
#define NAI_G1_OP_GET_AVAILABLE_RES      90003

/* Custom fields ŌĆö PIN_MAKE_FLD required for client extension */
#define NAI_G1_FLD_ERROR_CODE    PIN_MAKE_FLD(PIN_FLDT_INT, 55800)
#define NAI_G1_FLD_ERROR_DESCR   PIN_MAKE_FLD(PIN_FLDT_STR, 55801)

#define NAI_G1_FLD_NEXTAI_USAGE_G1 PIN_MAKE_FLD(PIN_FLDT_SUBSTRUCT, 10026)
#define NAI_G1_FLD_INPUT_TOKENS_G1 PIN_MAKE_FLD(PIN_FLDT_INT, 10022)
#define NAI_G1_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS_G1 PIN_MAKE_FLD(PIN_FLDT_INT, 10023)
#define NAI_G1_FLD_PROMPT_TEXT_G1  PIN_MAKE_FLD(PIN_FLDT_STR, 10024)
#define NAI_G1_FLD_MODEL_CODE_G1   PIN_MAKE_FLD(PIN_FLDT_STR, 10025)

#endif /* NAI_G1_PCM_EXT_H */

#ifndef NAI_G1_FLDS_H
#define NAI_G1_FLDS_H

#define NAI_G1_FLD_ERROR_CODE            55800
#define NAI_G1_FLD_ERROR_DESCR           55801

#define NAI_G1_FLD_NEXTAI_USAGE_G1       10026
#define NAI_G1_FLD_TRANS_ID              189
#define NAI_G1_FLD_INPUT_TOKENS_G1       10022
#define NAI_G1_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS_G1      10023
#define NAI_G1_FLD_PROMPT_TEXT_G1        10024
#define NAI_G1_FLD_MODEL_CODE_G1         10025

#define NAI_G1_ERR_SUCCESS               0
#define NAI_G1_ERR_BRM                   1
#define NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION            2

#endif /* NAI_G1_FLDS_H */

#include "nai_g1_common.h"
#include <string.h>

void
fm_nai_g1_pol_config(
    int32 *errp,
    pin_flist_t **config_flistpp,
    pin_errbuf_t *ebufp)
{
    pin_flist_t *flistp = NULL;

    if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
        *errp = PIN_ERR_BAD_ARG;
        return;
    }

    flistp = PIN_FLIST_CREATE(ebufp);
    PIN_ERR_LOG_MSG(PIN_ERR_LEVEL_WARNING, "FM_NAI_G1_POL: config loaded (stub)");

    if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
        *errp = PIN_ERR_BAD_ARG;
        PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&flistp, NULL);
        return;
    }

    *config_flistpp = flistp;
    *errp = 0;
}

int32
nai_g1_pol_model_to_product_family(const char *model_code)
{
    if (model_code == NULL) {
        return -1;
    }
    if (strcmp(model_code, NAI_G1_MODEL_ODDISEY_30) == 0) {
        return 30;
    }
    if (strcmp(model_code, NAI_G1_MODEL_ODDISEY_35) == 0) {
        return 35;
    }
    return -1;
}

#include "nai_g1_common.h"
#include <stdio.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <time.h>

const char *
nai_g1_ebuf_descr(pin_errbuf_t *ebufp)
{
    static char buf[256];

    if (ebufp == NULL) {
        return "BRM error";
    }
    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "BRM error %d", ebufp->pin_err);
    return buf;
}

void
nai_g1_set_error(pin_flist_t *out_flistp, int32 err_code,
    const char *err_descr, pin_errbuf_t *ebufp)
{
    if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp) || out_flistp == NULL) {
        return;
    }
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(out_flistp, NAI_G1_FLD_ERROR_CODE, &err_code, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(out_flistp, NAI_G1_FLD_ERROR_DESCR,
        (void *)(err_descr != NULL ? err_descr : ""), ebufp);
}

const char *
nai_g1_resource_name(int32 resource_id)
{
    switch (resource_id) {
    case NAI_G1_RES_PROMPT:
        return NAI_G1_RES_PROMPT_NAME;
    case NAI_G1_RES_TOKEN:
        return NAI_G1_RES_TOKEN_NAME;
    default:
        return "Unknown";
    }
}

pin_flist_t *
nai_g1_search_service(pcm_context_t *ctxp, const char *login, pin_errbuf_t *ebufp)
{
    pin_flist_t *s_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *args_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *r_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *svc_flistp = NULL;
    poid_t *search_poidp = NULL;
    int32 sflags = SRCH_DISTINCT;
    int64 db = 1;
    char *template =
        "select X from /service where F1 = V1 and service_t.poid_type = V2 ";

    if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp) || login == NULL || login[0] == '\0') {
        return NULL;
    }

    s_flistp = PIN_FLIST_CREATE(ebufp);
    search_poidp = PIN_POID_CREATE(db, "/search", -1, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_PUT(s_flistp, PIN_FLD_POID, search_poidp, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(s_flistp, PIN_FLD_FLAGS, &sflags, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(s_flistp, PIN_FLD_TEMPLATE, template, ebufp);

    args_flistp = PIN_FLIST_ELEM_ADD(s_flistp, PIN_FLD_ARGS, 1, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(args_flistp, PIN_FLD_LOGIN, (void *)login, ebufp);

    args_flistp = PIN_FLIST_ELEM_ADD(s_flistp, PIN_FLD_ARGS, 2, ebufp);
    {
        poid_t *type_poidp = PIN_POID_CREATE(db, NAI_G1_SERVICE_TYPE, -1, ebufp);
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_PUT(args_flistp, PIN_FLD_POID, type_poidp, ebufp);
    }

    PCM_OP(ctxp, PCM_OP_SEARCH, 0, s_flistp, &r_flistp, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&s_flistp, NULL);

    if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
        PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&r_flistp, NULL);
        return NULL;
    }

    svc_flistp = PIN_FLIST_ELEM_GET(r_flistp, PIN_FLD_RESULTS, PIN_ELEMID_ANY, 1, ebufp);
    if (svc_flistp == NULL) {
        PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&r_flistp, NULL);
        pin_set_err(ebufp, PIN_ERRLOC_FM, PIN_ERRCLASS_APPLICATION,
            NAI_G1_OP_ACT_RATE, 0, 0, PIN_ERR_NOT_FOUND);
        return NULL;
    }

    return r_flistp;
}

static int
nai_g1_subbal_is_valid(pin_flist_t *subbal_flistp, time_t now_t)
{
    pin_errbuf_t ebuf;
    time_t *valid_to = NULL;

    PIN_ERR_CLEAR_ERR(&ebuf);
    valid_to = (time_t *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(subbal_flistp, PIN_FLD_VALID_TO, 1, &ebuf);

    if (valid_to == NULL || *valid_to == 0) {
        return 1;
    }
    return (*valid_to >= now_t);
}

void
nai_g1_read_balgrp_sub_balances(pcm_context_t *ctxp, poid_t *bal_grp_poidp,
    pin_flist_t *results_flistp, pin_errbuf_t *ebufp)
{
    pin_flist_t *r_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *subbal_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *res_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *i_flistp = NULL;
    pin_decimal_t *amountp = NULL;
    pin_decimal_t *zerop = pbo_decimal_from_str("0.0", ebufp);
    int32 resource_id = 0;
    int32 rec_id = 0;
    int32 elem_id = 0;
    pin_cookie_t cookie = NULL;
    time_t now_t = pin_virtual_time((time_t *)NULL);

    if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp) || bal_grp_poidp == NULL || results_flistp == NULL) {
        return;
    }

    i_flistp = PIN_FLIST_CREATE(ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(i_flistp, PIN_FLD_POID, bal_grp_poidp, ebufp);

    PCM_OP(ctxp, PCM_OP_READ_OBJ, 0, i_flistp, &r_flistp, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&i_flistp, NULL);

    if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
        PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&r_flistp, NULL);
        pbo_decimal_destroy(&zerop);
        return;
    }

    while ((subbal_flistp = PIN_FLIST_ELEM_GET_NEXT(r_flistp, PIN_FLD_SUB_BALANCES,
            &elem_id, 1, &cookie, ebufp)) != NULL) {

        if (PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(subbal_flistp, PIN_FLD_RESOURCE_ID, 1, ebufp) == NULL) {
            continue;
        }
        resource_id = *(int32 *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(subbal_flistp, PIN_FLD_RESOURCE_ID, 1, ebufp);

        if (resource_id != NAI_G1_RES_PROMPT && resource_id != NAI_G1_RES_TOKEN) {
            continue;
        }
        if (!nai_g1_subbal_is_valid(subbal_flistp, now_t)) {
            continue;
        }

        amountp = (pin_decimal_t *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(subbal_flistp, PIN_FLD_CURRENT_BAL, 1, ebufp);
        if (amountp == NULL || pbo_decimal_compare(amountp, zerop, ebufp) <= 0) {
            continue;
        }

        res_flistp = PIN_FLIST_ELEM_ADD(results_flistp, PIN_FLD_RESULTS, rec_id, ebufp);
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(res_flistp, PIN_FLD_RESOURCE_ID, &resource_id, ebufp);
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(res_flistp, PIN_FLD_RESOURCE_NAME,
            (void *)nai_g1_resource_name(resource_id), ebufp);
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(res_flistp, PIN_FLD_AMOUNT, amountp, ebufp);
        rec_id++;
    }

    PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&r_flistp, NULL);
    pbo_decimal_destroy(&zerop);
}

/*
 * NextAI Group 1 custom opcodes (90001-90003).
 * Token block qty: pin_rum NAI_TOKEN on /event/session/usagegr1.
 */
#include "nai_g1_common.h"
#include <stdarg.h>
#include <stdio.h>
#include <string.h>

static void
nai_g1_log_msg(const char *fmt, ...)
{
    char buf[512];
    va_list ap;

    va_start(ap, fmt);
    vsnprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), fmt, ap);
    va_end(ap);
    PIN_ERR_LOG_MSG(PIN_ERR_LEVEL_WARNING, buf);
}

static int
nai_g1_str_empty(const char *s)
{
    return (s == NULL || s[0] == '\0');
}

static poid_t *
nai_g1_extract_service_poid(pin_flist_t *commit_out_flistp, pin_errbuf_t *ebufp)
{
    pin_flist_t *svc_flistp = NULL;
    poid_t *svc_poidp = NULL;
    int32 elem_id = 0;
    pin_cookie_t cookie = NULL;

    while ((svc_flistp = PIN_FLIST_ELEM_GET_NEXT(commit_out_flistp, PIN_FLD_SERVICES,
            &elem_id, 1, &cookie, ebufp)) != NULL) {
        svc_poidp = (poid_t *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(svc_flistp, PIN_FLD_POID, 1, ebufp);
        if (svc_poidp != NULL &&
            strcmp(PIN_POID_GET_TYPE(svc_poidp), NAI_G1_SERVICE_TYPE) == 0) {
            return svc_poidp;
        }
    }
    return NULL;
}

static void
nai_g1_truncate_prompt(char *dest, size_t dest_len, const char *src)
{
    if (dest == NULL || dest_len == 0) {
        return;
    }
    if (src == NULL) {
        dest[0] = '\0';
        return;
    }
    strncpy(dest, src, dest_len - 1);
    dest[dest_len - 1] = '\0';
}

void
op_nai_g1_act_commit_customer(
    cm_nap_connection_t *connp,
    u_int opcode,
    u_int flags,
    pin_flist_t *in_flistp,
    pin_flist_t **ret_flistpp,
    pin_errbuf_t *ebufp)
{
    pcm_context_t *ctxp = connp->dm_ctx;
    pin_flist_t *out_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *pcm_out_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *svc_flistp = NULL;
    poid_t *svc_typep = NULL;
    char *login = NULL;
    poid_t *svc_poidp = NULL;

    if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
        return;
    }
    PIN_ERR_CLEAR_ERR(ebufp);

    out_flistp = PIN_FLIST_CREATE(ebufp);
    nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_SUCCESS, "", ebufp);

    if (in_flistp == NULL) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION, "Missing input flist", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }
    if (PIN_FLIST_ELEM_COUNT(in_flistp, PIN_FLD_NAMEINFO, ebufp) == 0) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION, "Missing: PIN_FLD_NAMEINFO", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }
    if (PIN_FLIST_ELEM_COUNT(in_flistp, PIN_FLD_BILLINFO, ebufp) == 0) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION, "Missing: PIN_FLD_BILLINFO", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }
    if (PIN_FLIST_ELEM_COUNT(in_flistp, PIN_FLD_PAYINFO, ebufp) == 0) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION, "Missing: PIN_FLD_PAYINFO", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }
    if (PIN_FLIST_ELEM_COUNT(in_flistp, PIN_FLD_SERVICES, ebufp) == 0) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION, "Missing: PIN_FLD_SERVICES", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }

    svc_flistp = PIN_FLIST_ELEM_GET(in_flistp, PIN_FLD_SERVICES, PIN_ELEMID_ANY, 1, ebufp);
    svc_typep = (poid_t *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(svc_flistp, PIN_FLD_SERVICE_OBJ, 1, ebufp);
    if (svc_typep == NULL ||
        strcmp(PIN_POID_GET_TYPE(svc_typep), NAI_G1_SERVICE_TYPE) != 0) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION,
            "Invalid: PIN_FLD_SERVICE_OBJ must be /service/nextaig1v2", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }

    login = (char *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(svc_flistp, PIN_FLD_LOGIN, 1, ebufp);
    if (nai_g1_str_empty(login)) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION, "Missing: PIN_FLD_LOGIN", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }

    PCM_OP(ctxp, PCM_OP_CUST_COMMIT_CUSTOMER, 0, in_flistp, &pcm_out_flistp, ebufp);
    if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_BRM, nai_g1_ebuf_descr(ebufp), ebufp);
        PIN_ERR_CLEAR_ERR(ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }

    svc_poidp = nai_g1_extract_service_poid(pcm_out_flistp, ebufp);
    if (svc_poidp == NULL) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_BRM,
            "Commit succeeded but /service/nextaig1v2 POID not found", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }

    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(out_flistp, PIN_FLD_POID, svc_poidp, ebufp);
    nai_g1_log_msg("NAI_OP_COMMIT_CUSTOMER success login=%s", login);

cleanup:
    PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&pcm_out_flistp, NULL);
    if (ret_flistpp) {
        *ret_flistpp = out_flistp;
    } else {
        PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&out_flistp, NULL);
    }
    (void)opcode;
    (void)flags;
}

void
op_nai_g1_act_rate(
    cm_nap_connection_t *connp,
    u_int opcode,
    u_int flags,
    pin_flist_t *in_flistp,
    pin_flist_t **ret_flistpp,
    pin_errbuf_t *ebufp)
{
    pcm_context_t *ctxp = connp->dm_ctx;
    pin_flist_t *out_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *search_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *svc_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *session_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *usage_g1p = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *rate_out_flistp = NULL;
    poid_t *svc_poidp = NULL;
    poid_t *acct_poidp = NULL;
    poid_t *event_poidp = NULL;
    char *login = NULL;
    char *txn_id = NULL;
    char *model = NULL;
    char *prompt = NULL;
    char prompt255[256];
    int32 *in_tokens = NULL;
    int32 *out_tokens = NULL;
    time_t now_t = pin_virtual_time((time_t *)NULL);
    int64 db = 1;

    if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
        return;
    }
    PIN_ERR_CLEAR_ERR(ebufp);

    out_flistp = PIN_FLIST_CREATE(ebufp);
    nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_SUCCESS, "", ebufp);

    login = (char *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(in_flistp, PIN_FLD_LOGIN, 1, ebufp);
    txn_id = (char *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(in_flistp, NAI_G1_FLD_TRANS_ID, 1, ebufp);
    model = (char *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(in_flistp, NAI_G1_FLD_MODEL_CODE_G1, 1, ebufp);
    in_tokens = (int32 *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(in_flistp, NAI_G1_FLD_INPUT_TOKENS_G1, 1, ebufp);
    out_tokens = (int32 *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(in_flistp, NAI_G1_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS_G1, 1, ebufp);
    prompt = (char *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(in_flistp, NAI_G1_FLD_PROMPT_TEXT_G1, 1, ebufp);

    if (nai_g1_str_empty(login)) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION, "Missing: PIN_FLD_LOGIN", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }
    if (nai_g1_str_empty(txn_id)) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION, "Missing: PIN_FLD_TRANS_ID", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }
    if (nai_g1_str_empty(model)) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION, "Missing: PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE_G1", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }
    if (strcmp(model, NAI_G1_MODEL_ODDISEY_30) != 0 &&
        strcmp(model, NAI_G1_MODEL_ODDISEY_35) != 0) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION, "Invalid: PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE_G1", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }
    if (in_tokens == NULL || out_tokens == NULL || *in_tokens < 0 || *out_tokens < 0) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION,
            "Invalid: token counts must be >= 0", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }

    search_flistp = nai_g1_search_service(ctxp, login, ebufp);
    if (search_flistp == NULL || PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
        PIN_ERR_CLEAR_ERR(ebufp);
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION, "Login not found", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }

    svc_flistp = PIN_FLIST_ELEM_GET(search_flistp, PIN_FLD_RESULTS, PIN_ELEMID_ANY, 1, ebufp);
    svc_poidp = (poid_t *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(svc_flistp, PIN_FLD_POID, 1, ebufp);
    acct_poidp = (poid_t *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(svc_flistp, PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ, 1, ebufp);

    session_flistp = PIN_FLIST_CREATE(ebufp);
    event_poidp = PIN_POID_CREATE(db, NAI_G1_EVENT_USAGE_TYPE, -1, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_PUT(session_flistp, PIN_FLD_POID, event_poidp, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(session_flistp, PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ, acct_poidp, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(session_flistp, PIN_FLD_SERVICE_OBJ, svc_poidp, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(session_flistp, PIN_FLD_PROGRAM_NAME, NAI_G1_OP_ACT_RATE_STR, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(session_flistp, PIN_FLD_START_T, &now_t, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(session_flistp, PIN_FLD_END_T, &now_t, ebufp);

    usage_g1p = PIN_FLIST_SUBSTR_ADD(session_flistp, NAI_G1_FLD_NEXTAI_USAGE_G1, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(usage_g1p, NAI_G1_FLD_TRANS_ID, txn_id, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(usage_g1p, NAI_G1_FLD_INPUT_TOKENS_G1, in_tokens, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(usage_g1p, NAI_G1_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS_G1, out_tokens, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(usage_g1p, NAI_G1_FLD_MODEL_CODE_G1, model, ebufp);
    nai_g1_truncate_prompt(prompt255, sizeof(prompt255), prompt);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(usage_g1p, NAI_G1_FLD_PROMPT_TEXT_G1, prompt255, ebufp);

    PCM_OP(ctxp, PCM_OP_ACT_LOAD_SESSION, 0, session_flistp, &rate_out_flistp, ebufp);
    if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_BRM, nai_g1_ebuf_descr(ebufp), ebufp);
        nai_g1_log_msg("NAI_OP_ACT_RATE failed login=%s txn=%s", login, txn_id);
        PIN_ERR_CLEAR_ERR(ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }

    event_poidp = (poid_t *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(rate_out_flistp, PIN_FLD_POID, 1, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(out_flistp, PIN_FLD_POID, event_poidp, ebufp);
    nai_g1_log_msg("NAI_OP_ACT_RATE success login=%s txn=%s", login, txn_id);

cleanup:
    PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&search_flistp, NULL);
    PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&session_flistp, NULL);
    PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&rate_out_flistp, NULL);
    if (ret_flistpp) {
        *ret_flistpp = out_flistp;
    } else {
        PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&out_flistp, NULL);
    }
    (void)opcode;
    (void)flags;
}

void
op_nai_g1_act_get_available_res(
    cm_nap_connection_t *connp,
    u_int opcode,
    u_int flags,
    pin_flist_t *in_flistp,
    pin_flist_t **ret_flistpp,
    pin_errbuf_t *ebufp)
{
    pcm_context_t *ctxp = connp->dm_ctx;
    pin_flist_t *out_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *search_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *svc_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *svc_read_flistp = NULL;
    pin_flist_t *svc_read_out_flistp = NULL;
    poid_t *svc_poidp = NULL;
    poid_t *bal_grp_poidp = NULL;
    char *login = NULL;

    if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
        return;
    }
    PIN_ERR_CLEAR_ERR(ebufp);

    out_flistp = PIN_FLIST_CREATE(ebufp);
    nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_SUCCESS, "", ebufp);

    login = (char *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(in_flistp, PIN_FLD_LOGIN, 1, ebufp);
    if (nai_g1_str_empty(login)) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION, "Missing: PIN_FLD_LOGIN", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }

    search_flistp = nai_g1_search_service(ctxp, login, ebufp);
    if (search_flistp == NULL || PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
        PIN_ERR_CLEAR_ERR(ebufp);
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_VALIDATION, "Login not found", ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }

    svc_flistp = PIN_FLIST_ELEM_GET(search_flistp, PIN_FLD_RESULTS, PIN_ELEMID_ANY, 1, ebufp);
    svc_poidp = (poid_t *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(svc_flistp, PIN_FLD_POID, 1, ebufp);

    svc_read_flistp = PIN_FLIST_CREATE(ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(svc_read_flistp, PIN_FLD_POID, svc_poidp, ebufp);
    PCM_OP(ctxp, PCM_OP_READ_OBJ, 0, svc_read_flistp, &svc_read_out_flistp, ebufp);
    PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&svc_read_flistp, NULL);

    if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_BRM, nai_g1_ebuf_descr(ebufp), ebufp);
        PIN_ERR_CLEAR_ERR(ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }

    bal_grp_poidp = (poid_t *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(svc_read_out_flistp, PIN_FLD_BAL_GRP_OBJ, 1, ebufp);
    nai_g1_read_balgrp_sub_balances(ctxp, bal_grp_poidp, out_flistp, ebufp);
    if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
        nai_g1_set_error(out_flistp, NAI_G1_ERR_BRM, nai_g1_ebuf_descr(ebufp), ebufp);
        PIN_ERR_CLEAR_ERR(ebufp);
        goto cleanup;
    }

    nai_g1_log_msg("NAI_OP_GET_AVAILABLE_RES success login=%s", login);

cleanup:
    PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&search_flistp, NULL);
    PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&svc_read_out_flistp, NULL);
    if (ret_flistpp) {
        *ret_flistpp = out_flistp;
    } else {
        PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&out_flistp, NULL);
    }
    (void)opcode;
    (void)flags;
}

/*
 * PortalDevKit / Congero: opcode map via cm_fm_config (not PIN_FLD_OPCODES flist).
 * CM pin.conf: - cm fm_module ${PIN_HOME}/lib/libnai_g1_fm.so fm_nai_g1_act_config - pin
 */
struct cm_fm_config fm_nai_g1_act_config[] = {
    { NAI_G1_OP_COMMIT_CUSTOMER, "op_nai_g1_act_commit_customer" },
    { NAI_G1_OP_ACT_RATE, "op_nai_g1_act_rate" },
    { NAI_G1_OP_GET_AVAILABLE_RES, "op_nai_g1_act_get_available_res" },
    { 0, (char *)0 }
};

# Build from $PIN_HOME/sys/test/nextaig1 or source/apps/nextai_g1
PIN_HOME ?= /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM
OS ?= linux

# Congero lab: headers in PortalDevKit/include (not $PIN_HOME/include)
ifneq ($(wildcard $(PIN_HOME)/PortalDevKit/include/pcm.h),)
  INCDIR ?= $(PIN_HOME)/PortalDevKit/include
else
  INCDIR ?= $(PIN_HOME)/include
endif
LIBDIR ?= $(PIN_HOME)/lib

# cm_fm_config grows across BRM versions; opcode+name is the portable Oracle pattern
CFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -Wno-missing-field-initializers -fPIC -I$(INCDIR) -I./include
LDFLAGS = -shared -L$(LIBDIR)

SRCS = nai_g1_common.c nai_g1_act.c FM_NAI_G1_POL.c
OBJS = $(SRCS:.c=.o)

ifeq ($(OS),linux)
  LIB = libnai_g1_fm.so
else
  LIB = libnai_g1_fm.sl
endif

.PHONY: all clean install

all: $(LIB)

# FM .so: symbols resolved at CM load time ŌĆö do not link -lpcm/-lpin (Oracle FM pattern)
$(LIB): $(OBJS)
	$(CC) $(LDFLAGS) -o $@ $(OBJS) -lm

%.o: %.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

install: $(LIB)
	cp $(LIB) $(PIN_HOME)/lib/

clean:
	rm -f $(OBJS) $(LIB)

#!/bin/bash
# Register NextAI G1 opcodes/fields for testnap (sys/test pin.conf).
# CM pin.conf fm_module is separate ŌĆö both are required for xop 90001-90003.
set -e

PIN_HOME="${PIN_HOME:-/opt/app/brm/pin/BRM}"
INCDIR="${INCDIR:-$PIN_HOME/PortalDevKit/include}"
SRCDIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
TESTDIR="${TESTDIR:-$PIN_HOME/sys/test}"
EXT_H="$SRCDIR/include/nai_g1_pcm_ext.h"
EXT_OUT="$TESTDIR/nai_g1_ops_flds_ext"
PARSER="$(command -v parse_custom_ops_fields \
  || find "$PIN_HOME/bin" -name 'parse_custom_ops_fields*' 2>/dev/null | head -1)"

if [ ! -f "$EXT_H" ]; then
  echo "Missing $EXT_H" >&2
  exit 1
fi
if [ -z "$PARSER" ] || [ ! -x "$PARSER" ]; then
  echo "parse_custom_ops_fields not found under $PIN_HOME/bin" >&2
  exit 1
fi

export CPPFLAGS="-I$INCDIR -I$SRCDIR/include"
"$PARSER" -L C -I "$EXT_H" -O "$EXT_OUT"
echo "Created $EXT_OUT"
echo ""
echo "Add to $TESTDIR/pin.conf (client ŌĆö not CM pin.conf):"
echo "- - ops_fields_extension_file $EXT_OUT"

␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀hĆ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀pĆ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀xĆ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ĆĆ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀łĆ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ÉĆ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀śĆ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀"␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ĀĆ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀©Ć␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀#␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀░Ć␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ĖĆ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀└Ć␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀&␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀╚Ć␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ąĆ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀žĆ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ÓĆ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ĶĆ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀­Ć␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀°Ć␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀'␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ü␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀¾·HāņHŗčo␀␀Hģ└t ąHā─├␀␀␀␀␀ 5Ōo␀␀ %õo␀␀@␀ %Ōo␀␀h␀␀␀␀ķÓ    %┌o␀␀h␀␀␀ķą    %ęo␀␀h␀␀␀ķ└    %╩o␀␀h␀␀␀ķ░    %┬o␀␀h␀␀␀ķĀ    %║o␀␀h␀␀␀ķÉ    %▓o␀␀h␀␀␀ķĆ    %¬o␀␀h␀␀␀ķp    %óo␀␀h␀␀␀ķ`    %Üo␀␀h	␀␀␀ķP    %Æo␀␀h

Ė␀␀␀␀ķW␀␀Hā}ł␀t3HŗEłŗ@ģ└u(HŗEłŪ@<␀␀␀HŗEłHŹŁJ␀␀HēP HŗEłHēŪĶ&¹  ļĖ␀␀␀␀HēE░Hā}ł␀tHHŗEłŗ@ģ└u=HŗEłŪ@=␀␀␀HŗEłHŹjJ␀␀HēP HŗUłHŗEÓHēčHŪ┬    HŹ5K␀␀HēŪĶ~·  ļĖ␀␀␀␀HēEĶHā}ł␀tqHŗEłŗ@ģ└ufHŗEłŪ@>␀␀␀HŗEłHŹJ␀␀HēP HŗE░HŗMłHŗUĶIē╔Iēą╣␀␀␀␀║␀␀␀␀Š␀␀HēŪĶ~·  HŗEłŗ@ģ└t.HŗEĶ║␀␀␀␀Š␀␀HēŪĶ]¹  ļHŗEĶ║␀␀␀␀Š␀␀HēŪĶE¹  Hā}ł␀tNHŗEłŗ@ģ└uCHŗEłŪ@?␀␀␀HŗEłHŹäI␀␀HēP HŗE░HŗMłHŹUżIē╔Iēą╣␀␀␀␀║␀␀␀␀Š4␀␀HēŪĶ0·  Hā}ł␀tNHŗEłŗ@ģ└uCHŗEłŪ@@␀␀␀HŗEłHŹ/I␀␀HēP HŗE░HŗMłHŗUžIē╔Iēą╣␀␀␀␀║␀␀␀␀ŠD␀␀HēŪĶ█∙  Hā}ł␀tDHŗEłŗ@ģ└u9HŗEłŪ@B␀␀␀HŗEłHŹ┌H␀␀HēP HŗE░HŗUłHēč║␀␀␀Š1␀␀	HēŪĶr∙  ļĖ␀␀␀␀HēE°Hā}ł␀tNHŗEłŗ@ģ└uCHŗEłŪ@C␀␀␀HŗEłHŹåH␀␀HēP HŗMłHŗUÉHŗE°Iē╔Iēą╣␀␀␀␀║␀␀␀␀Š\␀␀HēŪĶ2∙  Hā}ł␀tDHŗEłŗ@ģ└u9HŗEłŪ@E␀␀␀HŗEłHŹ1H␀␀HēP HŗE░HŗUłHēč║␀␀␀Š1␀␀	HēŪĶ╔°  ļĖ␀␀␀␀HēE°Hā}ł␀tHHŗEłŗ@ģ└u=HŗEłŪ@G␀␀␀HŗEłHŹ▌G␀␀HēP HŗUłHŗEÓHēčHŪ┬    HŹ5īH␀␀HēŪĶ±„  ļĖ␀␀␀␀HēEąHā}ł␀tqHŗEłŗ@ģ└ufHŗEłŪ@H␀␀␀HŗEłHŹģG␀␀HēP HŗMłHŗUąHŗE°Iē╔Iēą╣␀␀␀␀║␀␀␀␀Š␀␀HēŪĶ±„  HŗEłŗ@ģ└t.HŗEą║␀␀␀␀Š␀␀HēŪĶą°  ļHŗEą║␀␀␀␀Š␀␀HēŪĶĖ°  Hā}ł␀tPHŗEłŗ@ģ└uEHŗEłŪ@K␀␀␀HŗEłHŹ„F␀␀HēP HŗU░HŗułHŹM©HŗEśIē±Iē╚Hēč║␀␀␀␀Š␀␀␀HēŪĶA„  HŗE░Hģ└tdHŪE╚␀␀␀␀Hā}╚␀uHŗE░HŗU╚HēųHēŪĶµ„  ļ8HŗE╚ŗ@ģ└u-HŗE╚Ū@L␀␀␀HŗE╚HŹzF␀␀HēP HŗE░HŗU╚HēųHēŪĶ¼„  HŪE░␀␀␀␀Hā}ł␀tHŗEłŗ@ģ└Ģ└ļ:Hŗ f␀␀Hŗ␀AĖN␀␀␀HŹ/F␀␀HēčHŹ.G␀␀HŹ5/F␀␀HēŪĖ␀␀␀␀Ķ

HŗEžHēŪĶ©┐  ļĖ␀␀␀␀HēE­╣␀␀␀HŹ±␀␀Hē┬HŹ/␀␀HēŲ┐␀␀␀Ķ└  Hā}ž␀tHŗEžŗ@ģ└Ģ└ļ:HŗČ.␀␀Hŗ␀AĖ␀␀␀HŹ«␀␀HēčHŹ$␀␀HŹ5Ł␀␀HēŪĖ␀␀␀␀ĶĀ┐  ģ└Ģ└ä└tyHŗEĶŪ␀␀␀␀HŗE­Hģ└t}HŪE°␀␀␀␀Hā}°␀uHŗE­HŗU°HēųHēŪĶ▓┐  ļ8HŗE°ŗ@ģ└u-HŗE°Ū@␀␀␀HŗE°HŹ/␀␀HēP HŗE­HŗU°HēųHēŪĶx┐  HŪE­␀␀␀␀ļHŗU­HŗEÓHēHŗEĶŪ␀␀␀␀␀ļÉ╔├UHēÕHāņHē}°Hā}°␀uĖ    ļGHŗE°HŹ;␀␀HēųHēŪĶ─Š  ģ└uĖ␀␀␀ļ&HŗE°HŹ␀␀HēųHēŪĶŻŠ  ģ└uĖ#␀␀␀ļĖ    ╔├␀¾·HāņHā─├␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ ü␀␀␀␀␀␀ q␀␀␀␀␀␀Ó␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ņ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀é␀␀␀␀␀␀␀r␀␀␀␀␀␀ ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀±␀␀␀␀␀␀0␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀r␀␀␀␀␀␀4␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀·␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀Xs␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀#␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀	␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀`z␀␀␀␀␀␀ž␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀8␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀#define NAI_OP_COMMIT_CUSTOMER 280214
#define NAI_OP_GET_AVAILABLE_RES 280213
#define NAI_OP_ACT_RATE 280215


#define PIN_FLD_INPUT_TOKENS_G1               PIN_MAKE_FLD(PIN_FLDT_INT, 10022)               /* input tokens */
#define PIN_FLD_NEXTAI_USAGE_G1          PIN_MAKE_FLD(PIN_FLDT_SUBSTRUCT, 10026)
#define PIN_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS_G1              PIN_MAKE_FLD(PIN_FLDT_INT, 10023)
#define PIN_FLD_PROMPT_TEXT_G1              PIN_MAKE_FLD(PIN_FLDT_STR, 10024)
#define PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE_G1              PIN_MAKE_FLD(PIN_FLDT_STR, 10025)

{ NAI_OP_COMMIT_CUSTOMER, "op_nai_commit_customer" },
{ NAI_OP_GET_AVAILABLE_RES, "op_nai_get_available_res" },
{ NAI_OP_ACT_RATE, "op_nai_act_rate" },
{ 0,    (char *)0 }
};

fm_nai_config_func()
{
  return ((void *) (fm_nai_config));
}

/*
 * Notes:
 * All CREATE functions, like PIN_FLIST_CREATE or PIN_POID_CREATE does memory allocation, and PCM call too, so destroy after use
 * don't destroy our universal return FLIST

#ifndef lint
static const char Sccs_id[] = "@(#)% %";

/*******************************************************************
 * Contains the NAI_OP_act_rate operation.
 *******************************************************************/

#include <stdio.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <time.h>

#include <pcm.h>
#include "ops/act.h"
#include "ops/custom_fields.h"
#include <pinlog.h>

#define FILE_LOGNAME "fm_nai_act_rate.c(1)"

#include "ops/nai_cust_ops.h"
#include "cm_fm.h"
#include "pin_errs.h"

/*******************************************************************
 * Routines contained within.
 *******************************************************************/
EXPORT_OP void
op_nai_act_rate(
        cm_nap_connection_t     *connp,
        int32                   opcode,
        int32                   flags,
        pin_flist_t             *i_flistp,
        pin_flist_t             **r_flistpp,
        pin_errbuf_t            *ebufp);

static int64
convert_to_epoch(
        const char              *date_str);

static void search_login(
        pcm_context_t           *ctxp,
        int32                   flags,
        poid_t                  *a_pdp,
        char                    *login,
        pin_flist_t             **service_flist,
        poid_t                  **service_poid,
        poid_t                  **account_obj,
        pin_errbuf_t            *ebufp);

static void
fm_nai_act_rate(
        pcm_context_t           *ctxp,
        int32                   flags,
        poid_t                  *a_pdp,
        poid_t                  *account_obj,
        char                    *date,
        char                    *descr,
        char                    *model_code,
        char                    *trans_id,
        char                    *prompt_txt,
        int64                   *tokens_in,
        int64                   *tokens_out,
        pin_flist_t             **r_flistpp,
        pin_errbuf_t            *ebufp);


/*******************************************************************
 * Routines needed from elsewhere.
 *******************************************************************/


/*******************************************************************
 * Main routine for the NAI_OP_act_rate operation.
 *******************************************************************/
void
op_nai_act_rate(
        cm_nap_connection_t     *connp,
        int32                   opcode,
        int32                   flags,
        pin_flist_t             *i_flistp,
        pin_flist_t             **r_flistpp,
        pin_errbuf_t            *ebufp)
{
        pcm_context_t           *ctxp = connp->dm_ctx;
        poid_t                  *acct_poidp = NULL;

        if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp))
                return;
        PIN_ERR_CLEAR_ERR(ebufp);

        /***********************************************************
         * Null out results
         ***********************************************************/
        *r_flistpp = NULL;

        /***********************************************************
         * Insanity check.
         ***********************************************************/
        if (opcode != NAI_OP_ACT_RATE) {
                pin_set_err(ebufp, PIN_ERRLOC_FM,
                        PIN_ERRCLASS_SYSTEM_DETERMINATE,
                        PIN_ERR_BAD_OPCODE, 0, 0, opcode);
                PIN_ERR_LOG_EBUF(PIN_ERR_LEVEL_ERROR,
                        "op_nai_act_rate opcode error", ebufp);

                return;
        }

        /***********************************************************
         * Debut what we got.
         ***********************************************************/
        PIN_ERR_LOG_FLIST(PIN_ERR_LEVEL_DEBUG,
                "op_nai_act_rate input flist", i_flistp);

        /***********************************************************
         * Main rountine for this opcode
         ***********************************************************/

        char err_msg[256] = "Login not found";

        /*
         * PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ is not a required fields, but we use it to retrive the database number for the poids
         */ 
        poid_t * a_pdp = (poid_t *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(i_flistp, PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ, 0, ebufp);
        char * login = (char *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(i_flistp, PIN_FLD_LOGIN, 0, ebufp);
        // date will hold the actual date time in the format month-date-year and convert to epoch time
        char * date = (char *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(i_flistp, PIN_FLD_NAME, 0, ebufp);
        char * descr = (char *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(i_flistp, PIN_FLD_DESCR, 0, ebufp);
        char * model_code = (char *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(i_flistp, PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE, 0, ebufp);
        char * trans_id = (char *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(i_flistp, PIN_FLD_TRANS_ID, 0, ebufp);
        char * prompt_txt = (char *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(i_flistp, PIN_FLD_PROMPT_TXT, 0, ebufp);
        int64 * tokens_in = (int64 *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(i_flistp, PIN_FLD_TOKENS_IN, 0, ebufp);
        int64 * tokens_out = (int64 *)PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(i_flistp, PIN_FLD_TOKENS_OUT, 0, ebufp);

        /*
         * service_flist will hold the PIN_FLD_RESULTS 
         * and service_poid will hold the poid of the service (via TAKE)
         */ 
        void * service_poid = NULL;
        void * account_obj = NULL;
        pin_flist_t * service_flist = NULL;
        search_login(ctxp, flags, a_pdp, login, &service_flist, &service_poid, &account_obj, ebufp);

        /*
         * service_poid is in the form of 0.0.0.1 /service/my_service XXXXX 0
         * we can cast a char * to it and do a strstr command
         * strstr(word, pattern) returns a pointer to the first letter of pattern inside word,
         * granted pattern exists in word, else return NULL
         */
        char * pattern = "service/nextaig1v2";
        char * is_nextai = NULL;
        char *poid_str = NULL;
        int32 len = 0; // To store the length of the string representation

        if (service_poid) {
            PIN_POID_TO_STR((poid_t *)service_poid, &poid_str, &len, ebufp);
            if (!PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
                is_nextai = strstr(poid_str, pattern);
            }
        }

        /*
         * If the user is not registered as /service/nextaig1v2, or he just dne
         * we return error code 1 with a list of missing fields
         * Note: destroy the input flist since we're returning
         */ 
        if (!is_nextai) {
            pin_flist_t *r_flistp = PIN_FLIST_CREATE(ebufp);
            poid_t *err_poid = PIN_POID_CREATE(1, "/error", -1, ebufp);
            PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(r_flistp, PIN_FLD_POID, err_poid, ebufp);
            PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(r_flistp, PIN_FLD_ERROR_CODE,
                              (void *)"2", ebufp);
            PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(r_flistp, PIN_FLD_ERROR_DESCR,
                              (void *)err_msg, ebufp);
            *r_flistpp = r_flistp;

            PIN_ERR_LOG_EBUF(PIN_ERR_LEVEL_ERROR, err_msg, ebufp);
            PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&i_flistp, ebufp);
            return;
        }

        /*
         * Calling our act_rate
         */ 
        fm_nai_act_rate(ctxp, flags, a_pdp, account_obj, date, descr, model_code, trans_id, prompt_txt, tokens_in, tokens_out, r_flistpp, ebufp);

        /***********************************************************
         * Error?
         ***********************************************************/
        if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
                PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(r_flistpp, ebufp);
                PIN_ERR_LOG_EBUF(PIN_ERR_LEVEL_ERROR,
                        "op_nai_act_rate error", ebufp);
        } else {
                PIN_ERR_LOG_FLIST(PIN_ERR_LEVEL_DEBUG,
                        "op_nai_act_rate output flist", *r_flistpp);
        }

        PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&i_flistp, ebufp);
        PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&service_flist, ebufp);

        return;
}

/*******************************************************************
 * convert_to_epoch:
 *
 * This function takes a string in the format: mm-dd-yyyy
 * It returns the epoch time if success, else -1
 *
 *******************************************************************/
static int64
convert_to_epoch(
        const char              *date_str)
{
        int month, day, year;

        if (sscanf(date_str, "%d-%d-%d", &month, &day, &year) != 3) {
            return -1;
        }

        if (month < 1 || month > 12 || day < 1 || day > 31 || year < 1900) {
            return -1;
        }

            struct tm tm = {0};
            tm.tm_year = year - 1900;
            tm.tm_mon = month - 1;
            tm.tm_mday = day;
            tm.tm_hour = 0;
            tm.tm_min = 0;
            tm.tm_sec = 0;
            tm.tm_isdst = -1;

            time_t epoch = mktime(&tm);
            if (epoch == -1) {
                return -1;
            }

            return (int64) epoch;
}

/*******************************************************************
 * search_login:
 *
 *******************************************************************/
static void
search_login(
        pcm_context_t           *ctxp,
        int32                   flags,
        poid_t                  *a_pdp,
        char                    *login,
        pin_flist_t             **service_flist,
        poid_t                  **service_poid,
        poid_t                  **account_obj,
        pin_errbuf_t            *ebufp)
{

        // check and clear err buff
        if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp))
            return;
        PIN_ERR_CLEAR_ERR(ebufp);

        // create a search flist
        pin_flist_t *search_flist = PIN_FLIST_CREATE(ebufp);
        pin_flist_t *vp = NULL;

        pin_cookie_t cookie = NULL;
        pin_flist_t * ret_flist = NULL;
        int32 element_id;

        /*
         * Retrieve the database number
         */
        int64 db = PIN_POID_GET_DB(a_pdp);

        /*
         * Create a search object, set flags, template and the arguments
         */
        poid_t * pdp = PIN_POID_CREATE(db, "/search", (int64)-1, ebufp);
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_PUT(search_flist, PIN_FLD_POID, (void *)pdp, ebufp);

        u_int uint_val = SRCH_DISTINCT;
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(search_flist, PIN_FLD_FLAGS, (void *)&uint_val, ebufp);

        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(search_flist, PIN_FLD_TEMPLATE,
                          (void *) "select X from /service where F1 = V1 ", ebufp);

        vp = PIN_FLIST_ELEM_ADD(search_flist, PIN_FLD_ARGS, 1, ebufp);
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(vp, PIN_FLD_LOGIN, login, ebufp);

        /*
         * Create PIN_FLD_RESULTS and set a NULL poid in it
         */ 
        vp = PIN_FLIST_ELEM_ADD(search_flist, PIN_FLD_RESULTS, 0, ebufp);
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(vp, PIN_FLD_POID, (void *)NULL, ebufp);
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(vp, PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ, (void *)NULL, ebufp);
        // note, consider adding a pin_fld_account_obj

        PIN_ERR_LOG_FLIST(PIN_ERR_LEVEL_DEBUG,
                          "search_login search flist", search_flist);

        /*
         * Call the PCM_OP_SEARCH
         */
        PCM_OP(ctxp, PCM_OP_SEARCH, 0, search_flist, &ret_flist, ebufp);

        /*
         * Use an internal return flist for the PCM call, when we retrieve the fields
         * from PIN_FLD_RESULTS, use TAKE since we are destroying the return flist
         */
        cookie = NULL;
        vp = PIN_FLIST_ELEM_GET_NEXT(ret_flist, PIN_FLD_RESULTS, &element_id, 1, &cookie, ebufp);

        /*
         * In case PCM_OP_SEARCH fails/returns NULL, exit the function
         */
        if (ret_flist == NULL || vp == NULL) {
            PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&search_flist, NULL);

            if (ret_flist != NULL) {
                PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&ret_flist, NULL);
            }

            return;
        }

        *service_poid = PIN_FLIST_FLD_TAKE(vp, PIN_FLD_POID, 0, ebufp); 
        *account_obj = PIN_FLIST_FLD_TAKE(vp, PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ, 0, ebufp); 

        /*
         * Cleaning
         */
        PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&search_flist, NULL);
        PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&ret_flist, NULL);

        if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
            PIN_ERR_LOG_EBUF(PIN_ERR_LEVEL_ERROR,
                            "search_login error", ebufp);
        }

        return;
}

static void
fm_nai_act_rate(
        pcm_context_t           *ctxp,
        int32                   flags,
        poid_t                  *a_pdp,
        poid_t                  *account_obj,
        char                    *date,
        char                    *descr,
        char                    *model_code,
        char                    *trans_id,
        char                    *prompt_txt,
        int64                   *tokens_in,
        int64                   *tokens_out,
        pin_flist_t             **r_flistpp,
        pin_errbuf_t            *ebufp)
{
        /*
         * Some Notes:
         *    use PUT for poids that are from PIN_POID_CREATE
         *    certain functions requires int64, some int32, some needs pointers
         */

        int64 one = 1;
        int64 neg_one = -1;
        int64 zero = 0;

        if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
                PIN_ERR_LOG_MSG(PIN_ERR_LEVEL_ERROR, "ERROR BUFFER NOT EMPTY IN BAL FUNCTION");
                return;
        }
        PIN_ERR_CLEAR_ERR(ebufp);

        /*
         * If convert_to_epoch fails, it will return -1
         */
        int64 timestamp = convert_to_epoch(date);
        if (timestamp == -1) {
            *r_flistpp = PIN_FLIST_CREATE(ebufp);
            poid_t *err_poid = PIN_POID_CREATE(1, "/error", -1, ebufp);
            PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(*r_flistpp, PIN_FLD_POID, err_poid, ebufp);
            PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(*r_flistpp, PIN_FLD_ERROR_CODE, (void *) "1", ebufp);
            PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(*r_flistpp, PIN_FLD_ERROR_DESCR, (void *) "Invalid information: date (PIN_FLD_NAME)", ebufp);

            return;
        }

        int64 db = PIN_POID_GET_DB(a_pdp);

        pin_flist_t * cust_flist = PIN_FLIST_CREATE(ebufp);
        poid_t * pdp = NULL;

        // 0 PIN_FLD_POID POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /plan ACCOUNT_NUM 0
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(cust_flist, PIN_FLD_POID, (void *)account_obj, ebufp);

        // 0 PIN_FLD_OBJ_TYPE STR [0] "/usagegr1"
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(cust_flist, PIN_FLD_OBJ_TYPE, (void *) "/usagegr1", ebufp);

        // 0 PIN_FLD_PROGRAM_NAME STR [0] "load_session.nap"
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(cust_flist, PIN_FLD_PROGRAM_NAME, (void *) "load_session.nap", ebufp);

        // 0 PIN_FLD_FLAGS INT [0] 0
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(cust_flist, PIN_FLD_FLAGS, &zero, ebufp);

        // 0 PIN_FLD_START_T TSTAMP [0] (1756133389)
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(cust_flist, PIN_FLD_START_T, &timestamp, ebufp);
        // 0 PIN_FLD_END_T TSTAMP [0] (1756133389)
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(cust_flist, PIN_FLD_END_T, &timestamp, ebufp);

        // 0 PIN_FLD_DESCR STR [0] "MYDESCR"
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(cust_flist, PIN_FLD_DESCR, (void *)descr, ebufp);

        // 0 PIN_FLD_INHERITED_INFO SUBSTRUCT [0]
        pin_flist_t * inherited_info = PIN_FLIST_ELEM_ADD(cust_flist, PIN_FLD_INHERITED_INFO, 0, ebufp);

        // 1     PIN_FLD_NEXTAI SUBSTRUCT [0]
        pin_flist_t * nextai = PIN_FLIST_ELEM_ADD(inherited_info, PIN_FLD_NEXTAI, 0, ebufp);

        // 2         PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE STR [0] "3.0 or 3.5"
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(nextai, PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE, (void *)model_code, ebufp);

        // 2         PIN_FLD_TRANS_ID STR [0] "MYTRANSID"
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(nextai, PIN_FLD_TRANS_ID, (void *)trans_id, ebufp);

        // 2         PIN_FLD_PROMPT_TXT STR [0] "MYPROMPT"
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(nextai, PIN_FLD_PROMPT_TXT, (void *)prompt_txt, ebufp);

        // 2         PIN_FLD_TOKENS_IN INT [0] 205000
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(nextai, PIN_FLD_TOKENS_IN, (void *)tokens_in, ebufp);

        // 2         PIN_FLD_TOKENS_OUT INT [0] 200
        PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(nextai, PIN_FLD_TOKENS_OUT, (void *)tokens_out, ebufp);

        pin_flist_t * pcm_return_flist = PIN_FLIST_CREATE(ebufp);
        PCM_OP(ctxp, PCM_OP_ACT_LOAD_SESSION, flags, cust_flist, &pcm_return_flist, ebufp); //dont use r_flistpp

        *r_flistpp = PIN_FLIST_CREATE(ebufp);

        /*
         * If theres an error, sends error code 1 with the message
         * else, sends error code 0 and the poid
         */
        if (PIN_ERR_IS_ERR(ebufp)) {
            PIN_ERR_LOG_EBUF(PIN_ERR_LEVEL_ERROR,
                        "op_nai_read error", ebufp);
            PIN_ERR_LOG_EBUF(PIN_ERR_LEVEL_ERROR, "fm_nai_act_rate PCM_OP error", ebufp);

            PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(*r_flistpp, PIN_FLD_ERROR_CODE, (void *)"1", ebufp);
            PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(*r_flistpp, PIN_FLD_ERROR_DESCR, (void *)"Issue with PCM_OP_ACT_LOAD_SESSION", ebufp);
        } else {
            PIN_ERR_LOG_FLIST(PIN_ERR_LEVEL_DEBUG, "PCM_OP_ACT_LOAD_SESSION output", pcm_return_flist);

            poid_t *cust_poid = PIN_FLIST_FLD_GET(pcm_return_flist, PIN_FLD_POID, 0, ebufp);
                if (cust_poid != NULL) {
                    PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(*r_flistpp, PIN_FLD_POID, (void *)cust_poid, ebufp);
            }

            PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(*r_flistpp, PIN_FLD_ERROR_CODE, (void *) "0", ebufp);
            PIN_FLIST_FLD_SET(*r_flistpp, PIN_FLD_ERROR_DESCR, (void *) "Act rated successfully", ebufp);

        }

        PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&cust_flist, NULL);
        PIN_FLIST_DESTROY_EX(&pcm_return_flist, NULL);
        return;
}
