#************************************************************************
# Configuration file for the testnap utility
#
#
# You use testnap to manually interact with the database and to test your
# custom applications.
#
# This configuration file is automatically installed and configured with
# default values during Portal installation. You can edit this file to:
#   -- change the default values of the entries.
#   -- disable an entry by inserting a crosshatch (#) at the start of
#        the line.
#   -- enable a commented entry by removing the crosshatch (#).
# 
# Before you make any changes to this file, save a backup copy.
#
# When editing this file, follow the instructions in each section.
# For more information on the general syntax of configuration entries,
# see "Reference Guide to Portal Configuration Files" in the Portal
# online documentation.
#************************************************************************


#======================================================================
#
# You can edit this file to suit your specific configuration:
#  -- You can change the default values of an entry.
#  -- You can exclude an optional entry by adding the # symbol
#     at the beginning of the line.
#  -- You can include a commented entry by removing the # symbol.
#
# Before you make any changes to this file, save a backup copy.
#
# To edit this file, follow the instructions in the commented sections.
# For more information on the general syntax of configuration entries,
# see "Reference Guide to Portal Configuration Files" in the Portal
# online documentation.
#======================================================================

#=======================================================================
# ptr_virtual_time
# 
# Enables the use of pin_virtual_time to advance Portal time, and 
# specifies the shared file for use by all Portal mangers.
#
# The entry has 2 values
#
# #/Empty     	to disable / enable pin_virtual_time in all pin.conf files
#		default = #	(disable)
#
# <filepath> 	location and name of the pin_virtual_time file shared by all Portal managers.
#		default = ${PIN_HOME}/lib/pin_virtual_time_file
#
#=======================================================================
- - pin_virtual_time ${PIN_HOME}/lib/pin_virtual_time_file



#========================================================================
# cm_ptr
# 
# Specifies a pointer to the CM or CMMP. 
#
# Use a separate entry for each CM or CMMP. If testnap can't find the
# first CM or CMMP, it looks for the next in the list.
#
# Each entry includes three values:
# 
#     <protocol> = "ip", for this release
#     <host>     = the name or IP address of the computer running the
#                    CM or CMMP
#     <port>     = the TCP port number of the CM or CMMP on this computer
#
# The port number should match a corresponding cm_ports entry with
# the same port number in the CM or CMMP configuration file. The 
# default, 11960, is a commonly specified port for the CM or CMMP.
#========================================================================
- nap cm_ptr ip congero-brm-training 11960


#========================================================================
# logfile
#
# Specifies the path to the log file for the sample application
#========================================================================
- sample_act    logfile sample_act.pinlog
- sample_del    logfile sample_del.pinlog
- sample_app    logfile sample_app.pinlog 
- sample_search logfile sample_search.pinlog 
- sample_who    logfile sample_who.pinlog


#========================================================================
# login_type
#
# Specifies whether the login name and password are required.
#
# The value for this entry can be:
#
#    0 = Only a user ID is required.
#    1 = (Default) Both a name and a password are required.
#
# By default, CMs require a user login and password when requesting an open
# context using PCM_CONTEXT_OPEN. However, you can remove this authentication
# requirement by configuring the CM with a cm_login_module of
# cm_login_null.so
#========================================================================
- nap login_type 1


#========================================================================
# login_name
#
# Specifies the login name to use when testnap connects to the CM. 
#========================================================================
- nap login_name root.0.0.0.1


#========================================================================
# login_pw
#
# Specifies the password to use when testnap connects to the CM.
#========================================================================
#- nap login_pw   


#========================================================================
# userid
#
# Specifies the database number and service type for the Portal
# database. 
#
# The CM uses the database number to identify the Portal database 
# and to connect to the correct Data Manager. For connections that don't
# require a login name and password, the CM also passes the service
# type to the database.
#
# The database number, in the form 0.0.0.N, is the number assigned to 
# your Portal database when you installed the system. The default
# is 0.0.0.1.
#
# The service type is /service/pcm_client and the ID is set based on login_name.
# DO NOT change these values.
#========================================================================
- - userid 0.0.0.1 /service/pcm_client 1


#========================================================================
# enable_ssl
#
# Specifies whether TLS will be enabled for PCM connections
#
#    0 - TLS is disabled.
#    1 - TLS is enabled.
#
#========================================================================
- nap enable_ssl 0

- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/custom_mapping.m

# Define .dat file

1   PIN_FLD_LAST_NAME STR [0] "Pham"
0 PIN_FLD_RESULTS  ARRAY [0] allocated 3, used 3
1   PIN_FLD_POID      POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account/nameinfo -1 0
1   PIN_FLD_LAST_NAME STR  [0] ""
1   PIN_FLD_FIRST_NAME STR [0] ""
XXX
d 1
xop PCM_OP_SEARCH 0 1

import subprocess

# Define the status code for Closed (2)
CLOSED_STATUS = "2"

# Read the accounts file
with open("accounts.txt", "r") as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

# Extract unique account numbers
account_ids = []
for line in lines:
    if "/account" in line:
        parts = line.split()
        # Find the index of /account and get the next item (the ID)
        try:
            idx = parts.index("/account")
            account_ids.append(parts[idx + 1])
        except (ValueError, IndexError):
            continue

print(f"Found {len(account_ids)} accounts to close. Generating testnap commands...")

# Build the testnap command sequence
testnap_input = ""
for acc_id in account_ids:
    testnap_input += f"""
r << XXX
0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID 0.0.0.1 /account {acc_id} 0
0 PIN_FLD_STATUS_INFO   ARRAY allocated 20
1     PIN_FLD_STATUS     ENUM {CLOSED_STATUS}
XXX
xop 32 0 1
"""

# Execute via testnap
try:
    process = subprocess.Popen(
        ["testnap"], 
        stdin=subprocess.PIPE, 
        stdout=subprocess.PIPE, 
        stderr=subprocess.PIPE, 
        text=True
    )
    stdout, stderr = process.communicate(input=testnap_input)
    print("Execution complete. Check testnap logs for details.")
except FileNotFoundError:
    print("Error: 'testnap' utility not found in your current PATH. Are your BRM env variables set?")
r << EOF_DATA 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /search -1 0
0 PIN_FLD_FLAGS           INT [0] 256
0 PIN_FLD_TEMPLATE        STR [0] "select X from /bill 1, /account 2 where 1.F1 = 2.F2 and 2.F3 = V3 "
0 PIN_FLD_RESULTS       ARRAY [0] allocated 20, used 11
1     PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ     POID [0] NULL
1     PIN_FLD_BILL_NO          STR [0] NULL
1     PIN_FLD_BILLINFO_OBJ    POID [0] NULL
1     PIN_FLD_START_T         TSTAMP [0] (0)
1     PIN_FLD_END_T           TSTAMP [0] (0)
1     PIN_FLD_DUE_T           TSTAMP [0] (0)
1     PIN_FLD_CURRENT_TOTAL DECIMAL [0] 0.0
1     PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] NULL
0 PIN_FLD_ARGS          ARRAY [1] allocated 1, used 1
1     PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ     POID [0] NULL
0 PIN_FLD_ARGS          ARRAY [2] allocated 1, used 1
1     PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] NULL
0 PIN_FLD_ARGS          ARRAY [3] allocated 1, used 1
1     PIN_FLD_NAMEINFO       ARRAY [*]
2         PIN_FLD_LAST_NAME    STR [0] "n"
EOF_DATA
d 1
xop PCM_OP_SEARCH 0 1
d 1
#!/bin/sh

# Target file containing your raw POID dump
INPUT_FILE="accounts.txt"

# Verify the file exists
if [ ! -f "$INPUT_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Error: $INPUT_FILE not found."
    exit 1
fi

echo "Parsing accounts and streaming to testnap..."

# Open a pipeline directly to testnap
(
    # Loop line-by-line through the file
    while read -r line; do
        # Look for lines containing the /account pattern
        case "$line" in
            *"/account"*)
                # Extract the number that follows /account
                # This drops everything up to '/account ' and isolates the ID
                acc_id=$(echo "$line" | sed 's/.*\/account \([0-9]*\).*/\1/')
                
                # Check to confirm we captured a clean numeric ID
                if [ -n "$acc_id" ]; then
                    echo "r << XXX"
                    echo "0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account $acc_id 0"
                    echo "0 PIN_FLD_STATUS_INFO   ARRAY [0] allocated 20"
                    echo "1     PIN_FLD_STATUS     ENUM [0] 2"
                    echo "XXX"
                    echo "xop 32 0 1"
                fi
                ;;
        esac
    done < "$INPUT_FILE"
) | testnap

echo "Execution stream finished. Verify status modifications in your testnap log."
r << XXX 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /search -1 0
0 PIN_FLD_FLAGS           INT [0] 256
0 PIN_FLD_TEMPLATE        STR [0] "select X from /bill 1, /account 2 where 1.F1 = 2.F2 and 2.F3 = V3 "
0 PIN_FLD_RESULTS       ARRAY [0] allocated 20, used 11
1     PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ     POID [0] NULL
1     PIN_FLD_BILL_NO          STR [0] ""
1     PIN_FLD_BILLINFO_OBJ    POID [0] NULL
1     PIN_FLD_START_T         TSTAMP [0] (0)
1     PIN_FLD_END_T           TSTAMP [0] (0)
1     PIN_FLD_DUE_T           TSTAMP [0] (0)
1     PIN_FLD_CURRENT_TOTAL DECIMAL [0] 0.0
1     PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] NULL
0 PIN_FLD_ARGS          ARRAY [1] allocated 1, used 1
1     PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ     POID [0] NULL
0 PIN_FLD_ARGS          ARRAY [2] allocated 1, used 1
1     PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] NULL
0 PIN_FLD_ARGS          ARRAY [3] allocated 1, used 1
1     PIN_FLD_NAMEINFO       ARRAY [*]
2         PIN_FLD_LAST_NAME    STR [0] "Bui"
XXX

d 1
xop PCM_OP_SEARCH 0 1

#=======================================
#  Storable Class /service/nextaig1
#  NextAI Service Group 1
#
#  Subclass of /service ŌĆö no custom fields (v2 requirement).
#  Login: PIN_FLD_LOGIN on service_t.
#  Rated via /event/session/usageg1 + PCM_OP_ACT_LOAD_SESSION.
#
#  Deploy from $PIN_HOME/sys/test:
#    $PIN_HOME/bin/pin_deploy verify service_nextaig1.podl
#    $PIN_HOME/bin/pin_deploy create service_nextaig1.podl
#=======================================
STORABLE CLASS /service/nextaig1 {
	READ_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";
	WRITE_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";
	CREATE_ACCESS = "Any";
	DESCR = "NextAI AI engine service Group 1. Subclass of /service with no custom attributes. Login uses PIN_FLD_LOGIN. Rated via /event/session/usageg1.";
	IS_PARTITIONED = "0";
	EVENT_TYPE = "NONE";
}
#=======================================
#  Storable Class /service/nextaig1
#  Oracle implementation ŌĆö uses base service_t only
#=======================================
STORABLE CLASS /service/nextaig1 IMPLEMENTATION ORACLE7 {
}
#!/bin/bash
# Load usage via NAI_OP_ACT_RATE (280215) ŌĆö based on act_test.nap
#
#   cd $PIN_HOME/sys/test
#   bash load_usage_102.sh
#   bash load_usage_102.sh --from 102 --to 200 --count 5
#   bash load_usage_102.sh --from 150 --to 150 --count 10 --date 10-15-2026
#
# Default: users 102ŌĆō200, 5 runs each, random date Oct 9ŌĆōNov 9 2026, tokens 3000ŌĆō40000

set -e
set +H

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

USER_START=102
USER_END=200
COUNT=5
MIN_TOKENS=3000
MAX_TOKENS=40000
DATE_YEAR=2026
FIXED_DATE=""
MODEL_CODE="3"
LOG_DIR="usage_logs"
NAP_FILE="./nextaig1/nap/act_usage_bulk.nap"

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --from)       USER_START="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --to)         USER_END="$2";   shift 2 ;;
        --count)      COUNT="$2";      shift 2 ;;
        --min-tokens) MIN_TOKENS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --max-tokens) MAX_TOKENS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --date)       FIXED_DATE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --year)       DATE_YEAR="$2";  shift 2 ;;
        *) echo "Usage: $0 [--from N] [--to N] [--count N] [--min-tokens N] [--max-tokens N] [--date MM-DD-YYYY] [--year YYYY]"; exit 1 ;;
    esac
done

if [[ "$MIN_TOKENS" -gt "$MAX_TOKENS" ]]; then
    echo "min-tokens must be <= max-tokens"
    exit 1
fi

if [[ "$USER_START" -gt "$USER_END" ]]; then
    echo "--from must be <= --to"
    exit 1
fi

token_range=$((MAX_TOKENS - MIN_TOKENS + 1))
total_users=$((USER_END - USER_START + 1))
total_runs=$((total_users * COUNT))

random_tokens() {
    echo $((MIN_TOKENS + (RANDOM * 32768 + RANDOM) % token_range))
}

random_usage_date() {
    if [[ -n "$FIXED_DATE" ]]; then
        echo "$FIXED_DATE"
        return
    fi
    local start_epoch end_epoch span random_epoch
    start_epoch=$(date -d "10/09/${DATE_YEAR}" +%s)
    end_epoch=$(date -d "11/09/${DATE_YEAR}" +%s)
    span=$((end_epoch - start_epoch + 1))
    random_epoch=$((start_epoch + (RANDOM * 32768 + RANDOM) % span))
    date -d "@$random_epoch" +"%m-%d-%Y"
}

mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" nextaig1/nap

ok=0
fail=0
run_no=0

for i in $(seq "$USER_START" "$USER_END"); do
    login="nextaiuser$(printf '%03d' "$i")"
    user_idx=$((i - USER_START + 1))

    for run in $(seq 1 "$COUNT"); do
        run_no=$((run_no + 1))
        usage_date=$(random_usage_date)
        tokens_in=$(random_tokens)
        tokens_out=$(random_tokens)
        trans_id="TXN-${i}-$(printf '%03d' "$run")"
        out="${LOG_DIR}/${login}_run$(printf '%03d' "$run").out"

        cat > "$NAP_FILE" <<EOF
r << END 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] \$DB_NO /account 1 0
0 PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ    POID [0] \$DB_NO /account 1 0
0 PIN_FLD_PROGRAM_NAME      STR [0] "testnap"
0 PIN_FLD_LOGIN             STR [0] "${login}"
0 PIN_FLD_NAME              STR [0] "${usage_date}"
0 PIN_FLD_DESCR             STR [0] "Simulated usage ${run}/${COUNT}"
0 PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE_G1     STR [0] "${MODEL_CODE}"
0 PIN_FLD_TRANS_ID          STR [0] "${trans_id}"
0 PIN_FLD_PROMPT_TEXT_G1    STR [0] "What is billing?"
0 PIN_FLD_INPUT_TOKENS_G1   INT [0] ${tokens_in}
0 PIN_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS_G1  INT [0] ${tokens_out}
END
xop 280215 0 1
q
EOF

        echo "[user ${user_idx}/${total_users}] [run ${run}/${COUNT}] [${run_no}/${total_runs}] ${login} date=${usage_date} in=${tokens_in} out=${tokens_out} trans=${trans_id}"
        if testnap "$NAP_FILE" > "$out" 2>&1; then
            if grep -qE 'PIN_FLD_ERROR_CODE\s+STR \[0\] "0"' "$out"; then
                ok=$((ok + 1))
            else
                fail=$((fail + 1))
                echo "  failed (see ${out})"
            fi
        else
            fail=$((fail + 1))
            echo "  testnap error (see ${out})"
        fi
    done
done

echo "Done. users=${USER_START}-${USER_END} runs_per_user=${COUNT} success=${ok} failed=${fail} (logs in ${LOG_DIR}/)"

r << XXX 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /search 0 0
0 PIN_FLD_FLAGS           INT [0] 256
0 PIN_FLD_TEMPLATE        STR [0] "select X from /account where F1 = V1 "
0 PIN_FLD_ARGS          ARRAY [1] allocated 1, used 1
1    PIN_FLD_NAMEINFO   ARRAY [1] allocated 1, used 1
2       PIN_FLD_LAST_NAME STR [0] "Tran"
0 PIN_FLD_RESULTS       ARRAY [0] allocated 2, used 2
1    PIN_FLD_POID        POID [0] NULL
1    PIN_FLD_NAMEINFO   ARRAY [*] allocated 1, used 1
2       PIN_FLD_FIRST_NAME STR [0] ""
2       PIN_FLD_LAST_NAME  STR [0] ""
XXX
d 1
xop PCM_OP_SEARCH 0 1
#************************************************************************
# Configuration file for the testnap utility
#
#
# You use testnap to manually interact with the database and to test your
# custom applications.
#
# This configuration file is automatically installed and configured with
# default values during Portal installation. You can edit this file to:
#   -- change the default values of the entries.
#   -- disable an entry by inserting a crosshatch (#) at the start of
#        the line.
#   -- enable a commented entry by removing the crosshatch (#).
# 
# Before you make any changes to this file, save a backup copy.
#
# When editing this file, follow the instructions in each section.
# For more information on the general syntax of configuration entries,
# see "Reference Guide to Portal Configuration Files" in the Portal
# online documentation.
#************************************************************************


#======================================================================
#
# You can edit this file to suit your specific configuration:
#  -- You can change the default values of an entry.
#  -- You can exclude an optional entry by adding the # symbol
#     at the beginning of the line.
#  -- You can include a commented entry by removing the # symbol.
#
# Before you make any changes to this file, save a backup copy.
#
# To edit this file, follow the instructions in the commented sections.
# For more information on the general syntax of configuration entries,
# see "Reference Guide to Portal Configuration Files" in the Portal
# online documentation.
#======================================================================

#=======================================================================
# ptr_virtual_time
# 
# Enables the use of pin_virtual_time to advance Portal time, and 
# specifies the shared file for use by all Portal mangers.
#
# The entry has 2 values
#
# #/Empty     	to disable / enable pin_virtual_time in all pin.conf files
#		default = #	(disable)
#
# <filepath> 	location and name of the pin_virtual_time file shared by all Portal managers.
#		default = ${PIN_HOME}/lib/pin_virtual_time_file
#
#=======================================================================
- - pin_virtual_time ${PIN_HOME}/lib/pin_virtual_time_file



#========================================================================
# cm_ptr
# 
# Specifies a pointer to the CM or CMMP. 
#
# Use a separate entry for each CM or CMMP. If testnap can't find the
# first CM or CMMP, it looks for the next in the list.
#
# Each entry includes three values:
# 
#     <protocol> = "ip", for this release
#     <host>     = the name or IP address of the computer running the
#                    CM or CMMP
#     <port>     = the TCP port number of the CM or CMMP on this computer
#
# The port number should match a corresponding cm_ports entry with
# the same port number in the CM or CMMP configuration file. The 
# default, 11960, is a commonly specified port for the CM or CMMP.
#========================================================================
- nap cm_ptr ip 127.0.0.1 11960


#========================================================================
# logfile
#
# Specifies the path to the log file for the sample application
#========================================================================
- sample_act    logfile sample_act.pinlog
- sample_del    logfile sample_del.pinlog
- sample_app    logfile sample_app.pinlog 
- sample_search logfile sample_search.pinlog 
- sample_who    logfile sample_who.pinlog


#========================================================================
# login_type
#
# Specifies whether the login name and password are required.
#
# The value for this entry can be:
#
#    0 = Only a user ID is required.
#    1 = (Default) Both a name and a password are required.
#
# By default, CMs require a user login and password when requesting an open
# context using PCM_CONTEXT_OPEN. However, you can remove this authentication
# requirement by configuring the CM with a cm_login_module of
# cm_login_null.so
#========================================================================
- nap login_type 1


#========================================================================
# login_name
#
# Specifies the login name to use when testnap connects to the CM. 
#========================================================================
- nap login_name root.0.0.0.1


#========================================================================
# login_pw
#
# Specifies the password to use when testnap connects to the CM.
#========================================================================
#- nap login_pw   


#========================================================================
# userid
#
# Specifies the database number and service type for the Portal
# database. 
#
# The CM uses the database number to identify the Portal database 
# and to connect to the correct Data Manager. For connections that don't
# require a login name and password, the CM also passes the service
# type to the database.
#
# The database number, in the form 0.0.0.N, is the number assigned to 
# your Portal database when you installed the system. The default
# is 0.0.0.1.
#
# The service type is /service/pcm_client and the ID is set based on login_name.
# DO NOT change these values.
#========================================================================
- - userid 0.0.0.1 /service/pcm_client 1


#========================================================================
# enable_ssl
#
# Specifies whether TLS will be enabled for PCM connections
#
#    0 - TLS is disabled.
#    1 - TLS is enabled.
#
#========================================================================
- nap enable_ssl 0

# Group 3 Custom Field
#- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/g3_fields.dat

# Define .dat file
#- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/nextai_fields.dat

# Custom fields/opcodes dat file
#- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/combined.dat

# Load custom FM group 1
- cm fm_module /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/lib/fm_nai_custom.so fm_nai_config - pin


#========================================================================
# Group 4 add extension file
#- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/naig4_cust_ops.dat
#- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/bin/naig4_acc_cust_ops.dat
#========================================================================

#************************************************************************
# Configuration file for the testnap utility
#
#
# You use testnap to manually interact with the database and to test your
# custom applications.
#
# This configuration file is automatically installed and configured with
# default values during Portal installation. You can edit this file to:
#   -- change the default values of the entries.
#   -- disable an entry by inserting a crosshatch (#) at the start of
#        the line.
#   -- enable a commented entry by removing the crosshatch (#).
# 
# Before you make any changes to this file, save a backup copy.
#
# When editing this file, follow the instructions in each section.
# For more information on the general syntax of configuration entries,
# see "Reference Guide to Portal Configuration Files" in the Portal
# online documentation.
#************************************************************************


#======================================================================
#
# You can edit this file to suit your specific configuration:
#  -- You can change the default values of an entry.
#  -- You can exclude an optional entry by adding the # symbol
#     at the beginning of the line.
#  -- You can include a commented entry by removing the # symbol.
#
# Before you make any changes to this file, save a backup copy.
#
# To edit this file, follow the instructions in the commented sections.
# For more information on the general syntax of configuration entries,
# see "Reference Guide to Portal Configuration Files" in the Portal
# online documentation.
#======================================================================

#=======================================================================
# ptr_virtual_time
# 
# Enables the use of pin_virtual_time to advance Portal time, and 
# specifies the shared file for use by all Portal mangers.
#
# The entry has 2 values
#
# #/Empty     	to disable / enable pin_virtual_time in all pin.conf files
#		default = #	(disable)
#
# <filepath> 	location and name of the pin_virtual_time file shared by all Portal managers.
#		default = ${PIN_HOME}/lib/pin_virtual_time_file
#
#=======================================================================
- - pin_virtual_time ${PIN_HOME}/lib/pin_virtual_time_file



#========================================================================
# cm_ptr
# 
# Specifies a pointer to the CM or CMMP. 
#
# Use a separate entry for each CM or CMMP. If testnap can't find the
# first CM or CMMP, it looks for the next in the list.
#
# Each entry includes three values:
# 
#     <protocol> = "ip", for this release
#     <host>     = the name or IP address of the computer running the
#                    CM or CMMP
#     <port>     = the TCP port number of the CM or CMMP on this computer
#
# The port number should match a corresponding cm_ports entry with
# the same port number in the CM or CMMP configuration file. The 
# default, 11960, is a commonly specified port for the CM or CMMP.
#========================================================================
- nap cm_ptr ip congero-brm-training 11960


#========================================================================
# logfile
#
# Specifies the path to the log file for the sample application
#========================================================================
- sample_act    logfile sample_act.pinlog
- sample_del    logfile sample_del.pinlog
- sample_app    logfile sample_app.pinlog 
- sample_search logfile sample_search.pinlog 
- sample_who    logfile sample_who.pinlog


#========================================================================
# login_type
#
# Specifies whether the login name and password are required.
#
# The value for this entry can be:
#
#    0 = Only a user ID is required.
#    1 = (Default) Both a name and a password are required.
#
# By default, CMs require a user login and password when requesting an open
# context using PCM_CONTEXT_OPEN. However, you can remove this authentication
# requirement by configuring the CM with a cm_login_module of
# cm_login_null.so
#========================================================================
- nap login_type 1


#========================================================================
# login_name
#
# Specifies the login name to use when testnap connects to the CM. 
#========================================================================
- nap login_name root.0.0.0.1


#========================================================================
# login_pw
#
# Specifies the password to use when testnap connects to the CM.
#========================================================================
#- nap login_pw   


#========================================================================
# userid
#
# Specifies the database number and service type for the Portal
# database. 
#
# The CM uses the database number to identify the Portal database 
# and to connect to the correct Data Manager. For connections that don't
# require a login name and password, the CM also passes the service
# type to the database.
#
# The database number, in the form 0.0.0.N, is the number assigned to 
# your Portal database when you installed the system. The default
# is 0.0.0.1.
#
# The service type is /service/pcm_client and the ID is set based on login_name.
# DO NOT change these values.
#========================================================================
- - userid 0.0.0.1 /service/pcm_client 1


#========================================================================
# enable_ssl
#
# Specifies whether TLS will be enabled for PCM connections
#
#    0 - TLS is disabled.
#    1 - TLS is enabled.
#
#========================================================================
- nap enable_ssl 0

# Group 3 Custom Field
#- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/g3_fields.dat

# Define .dat file
- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/nextai_fields.dat

# Custom fields/opcodes dat file
- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/combined.dat

# Load custom FM group 1
- cm fm_module /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/lib/fm_nai_custom.so fm_nai_config - pin


#========================================================================
# Group 4 add extension file
- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/naig4_cust_ops.dat
- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/bin/naig4_acc_cust_ops.dat
#========================================================================

#************************************************************************
# Configuration file for the testnap utility
#
#
# You use testnap to manually interact with the database and to test your
# custom applications.
#
# This configuration file is automatically installed and configured with
# default values during Portal installation. You can edit this file to:
#   -- change the default values of the entries.
#   -- disable an entry by inserting a crosshatch (#) at the start of
#        the line.
#   -- enable a commented entry by removing the crosshatch (#).
# 
# Before you make any changes to this file, save a backup copy.
#
# When editing this file, follow the instructions in each section.
# For more information on the general syntax of configuration entries,
# see "Reference Guide to Portal Configuration Files" in the Portal
# online documentation.
#************************************************************************


#======================================================================
#
# You can edit this file to suit your specific configuration:
#  -- You can change the default values of an entry.
#  -- You can exclude an optional entry by adding the # symbol
#     at the beginning of the line.
#  -- You can include a commented entry by removing the # symbol.
#
# Before you make any changes to this file, save a backup copy.
#
# To edit this file, follow the instructions in the commented sections.
# For more information on the general syntax of configuration entries,
# see "Reference Guide to Portal Configuration Files" in the Portal
# online documentation.
#======================================================================

#=======================================================================
# ptr_virtual_time
# 
# Enables the use of pin_virtual_time to advance Portal time, and 
# specifies the shared file for use by all Portal mangers.
#
# The entry has 2 values
#
# #/Empty     	to disable / enable pin_virtual_time in all pin.conf files
#		default = #	(disable)
#
# <filepath> 	location and name of the pin_virtual_time file shared by all Portal managers.
#		default = ${PIN_HOME}/lib/pin_virtual_time_file
#
#=======================================================================
- - pin_virtual_time ${PIN_HOME}/lib/pin_virtual_time_file



#========================================================================
# cm_ptr
# 
# Specifies a pointer to the CM or CMMP. 
#
# Use a separate entry for each CM or CMMP. If testnap can't find the
# first CM or CMMP, it looks for the next in the list.
#
# Each entry includes three values:
# 
#     <protocol> = "ip", for this release
#     <host>     = the name or IP address of the computer running the
#                    CM or CMMP
#     <port>     = the TCP port number of the CM or CMMP on this computer
#
# The port number should match a corresponding cm_ports entry with
# the same port number in the CM or CMMP configuration file. The 
# default, 11960, is a commonly specified port for the CM or CMMP.
#========================================================================
- nap cm_ptr ip congero-brm-training 11960


#========================================================================
# logfile
#
# Specifies the path to the log file for the sample application
#========================================================================
- sample_act    logfile sample_act.pinlog
- sample_del    logfile sample_del.pinlog
- sample_app    logfile sample_app.pinlog 
- sample_search logfile sample_search.pinlog 
- sample_who    logfile sample_who.pinlog


#========================================================================
# login_type
#
# Specifies whether the login name and password are required.
#
# The value for this entry can be:
#
#    0 = Only a user ID is required.
#    1 = (Default) Both a name and a password are required.
#
# By default, CMs require a user login and password when requesting an open
# context using PCM_CONTEXT_OPEN. However, you can remove this authentication
# requirement by configuring the CM with a cm_login_module of
# cm_login_null.so
#========================================================================
- nap login_type 1


#========================================================================
# login_name
#
# Specifies the login name to use when testnap connects to the CM. 
#========================================================================
- nap login_name root.0.0.0.1


#========================================================================
# login_pw
#
# Specifies the password to use when testnap connects to the CM.
#========================================================================
#- nap login_pw   


#========================================================================
# userid
#
# Specifies the database number and service type for the Portal
# database. 
#
# The CM uses the database number to identify the Portal database 
# and to connect to the correct Data Manager. For connections that don't
# require a login name and password, the CM also passes the service
# type to the database.
#
# The database number, in the form 0.0.0.N, is the number assigned to 
# your Portal database when you installed the system. The default
# is 0.0.0.1.
#
# The service type is /service/pcm_client and the ID is set based on login_name.
# DO NOT change these values.
#========================================================================
- - userid 0.0.0.1 /service/pcm_client 1


#========================================================================
# enable_ssl
#
# Specifies whether TLS will be enabled for PCM connections
#
#    0 - TLS is disabled.
#    1 - TLS is enabled.
#
#========================================================================
- nap enable_ssl 0

# Group 3 Custom Field
#- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/g3_fields.dat

# Define .dat file
#- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/nextai_fields.dat

#!/bin/bash

# Configuration
OPCODE=20036
TESTNAP_DIR="$PIN_HOME/sys/test"
TEMP_FLIST="/tmp/temp_acct.flist"

# Full List of Account IDs
ACCOUNTS=(
603987 606002 609424 614272 608226 613890 609889 604144 604723 605810
609616 614080 608914 612226 610273 604808 605107 606194 609328 614176
609106 613058 609937 602952 604915 605834 607664 613984 606770 613154
610129 605224 604939 606026 607472 614368 607154 612962 609841 605608
604747 603690 607496 614032 606962 611298 610225 605416 605131 605098
607304 612176 606986 613522 610033 605464 604843 604906 607688 611888
606794 613714 610057 603224 605035 602906 608424 612272 607178 611378
607817 603608 604699 602714 608616 612080 609450 611762 609225 603320
605083 603098 608280 612104 609642 611570 606889 603512 602843 611912
605233 604164 609641 609603 602939 608664 604858 612296 605617 604548
)

echo "Starting bulk delete test for ${#ACCOUNTS[@]} accounts..."
echo "Using Opcode: $OPCODE"
echo "----------------------------------------"

if [ -z "$PIN_HOME" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: PIN_HOME environment variable is not set."
    exit 1
fi

cd "$TESTNAP_DIR" || exit 1

for ACCT in "${ACCOUNTS[@]}"; do
    # 1. Use standard single spaces to isolate parameters perfectly for testnap
    echo -e "0 PIN_FLD_POID POID 0.0.0.1 /account $ACCT 0" > "$TEMP_FLIST"

    # 2. Execute testnap
    OUTPUT=$(testnap <<EOF
r $TEMP_FLIST 1
xop $OPCODE 0 1
q
EOF
)

    # 3. Enhanced output verification to make sure parse errors are handled correctly
    if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -qi -E "ERROR|bad parse|invalid buffer|no object|PIN_ERR"; then
        echo "[FAIL] Account $ACCT: Processing failed."
        # Print out the exact error block from testnap for easier debugging
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -A 1 -B 1 -i -E "ERROR|bad parse"
    else
        echo "[OK]   Account $ACCT: Processed successfully."
    fi
done

# Cleanup
rm -f "$TEMP_FLIST"
echo "----------------------------------------"
echo "Batch process completed."
cat > nk.nap <<'EOF'
# 1. Search account by account number
r << XXX 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /search -1 0
0 PIN_FLD_FLAGS          INT [0] 256
0 PIN_FLD_TEMPLATE       STR [0] "select X from /account where F1 = V1 "
0 PIN_FLD_ARGS           ARRAY [1] allocated 20, used 1
1     PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_NO STR [0] "0.0.0.1-309256"
0 PIN_FLD_RESULTS        ARRAY [0] allocated 20, used 4
1     PIN_FLD_POID       POID [0] NULL poid
1     PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_NO STR [0] ""
1     PIN_FLD_FIRST_NAME STR [0] ""
1     PIN_FLD_LAST_NAME  STR [0] ""
XXX
xop PCM_OP_SEARCH 0 1

# 2. Search bills for account
r << XXX 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID              POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /search -1 0
0 PIN_FLD_FLAGS             INT [0] 256
0 PIN_FLD_TEMPLATE          STR [0] "select X from /bill where F1 = V1 "
0 PIN_FLD_ARGS              ARRAY [1] allocated 20, used 1
1     PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ   POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account 309256 0
0 PIN_FLD_RESULTS           ARRAY [0] allocated 20, used 7
1     PIN_FLD_POID          POID [0] NULL poid
1     PIN_FLD_BILL_NO       STR [0] ""
1     PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ   POID [0] NULL poid
1     PIN_FLD_BILLINFO_OBJ  POID [0] NULL poid
1     PIN_FLD_START_T       TSTAMP [0] (0)
1     PIN_FLD_END_T         TSTAMP [0] (0)
1     PIN_FLD_CURRENT_TOTAL DECIMAL [0] 0
XXX
xop PCM_OP_SEARCH 0 1

# 3. Read account object
r << XXX 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account 309256 0
XXX
xop PCM_OP_READ_OBJ 0 1
EOF

#=======================================
#  Field PIN_FLD_ARGS
#=======================================

ARRAY PIN_FLD_ARGS {

	ID = 49;
}

	ID = 17;

#!/bin/bash

INPUT_CSV="customers.csv"
OUTPUT_CSV="logins.csv"

# Safety checks
if [[ ! -f "$OUTPUT_CSV" ]]; then
    echo "LOGIN" > "$OUTPUT_CSV"  # add header to csv if header DNE
fi

# loop over the rows and get the logins
tail -n +2 "$INPUT_CSV" | while IFS=',' read -r LOGIN _
do
    # append to logins.csv
    echo "$LOGIN" >> "$OUTPUT_CSV"
done

echo "Logins appended to $OUTPUT_CSV."

#  Storable Class /service/nextAI_gr04
#=======================================

STORABLE CLASS /service/nextAI_gr04 {

	READ_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";
	WRITE_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";
	DESCR = "Provides dialup Internet access. Includes connection information, such as IP address and connection protocol.";
	IS_PARTITIONED = "0";
	EVENT_TYPE = "NONE";

	#===================
	#  Fields 
	#===================

	ARRAY PIN_FLD_ARGS {

		DESCR = "Terminal Server Protocol Extensions Array. List of (name, value) pairs which are used to set the IP session characteristics. Element id not significant.  MultnextAI_gr04le entries with same name may be used if appropriate. See PCM_OP_TERM_POL_AUTHORIZE.";
		ORDER = 0;
		AUDITABLE = 0;
		ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
		SERIALIZABLE = 0;

		#===================
		#  Fields 
		#===================

		STRING PIN_FLD_NAME {

			DESCR = "Extension name. For RADIUS, this name must match a name from the dictionary. e.g. Framed-IP-Netmask, Framed-MTU.";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 255;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_VALUE {

			DESCR = "Extension value. Stringified version of the value for the corresponding extension name. e.g. for IP addrs this is in dotted decimal notation. For enumerated values, either enumerated name or decimal value may be used (e.g. PPP or 1 for Framed-Protocol)";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 1023;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

	}

}

#=======================================
#  Storable Class /service/ip
#=======================================

STORABLE CLASS /service/ip {

	READ_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";
	WRITE_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";
	DESCR = "Provides dialup Internet access. Includes connection information, such as IP address and connection protocol.";
	IS_PARTITIONED = "0";
	EVENT_TYPE = "NONE";

	#===================
	#  Fields 
	#===================

	ARRAY PIN_FLD_ARGS {

		DESCR = "Terminal Server Protocol Extensions Array. List of (name, value) pairs which are used to set the IP session characteristics. Element id not significant.  Multiple entries with same name may be used if appropriate. See PCM_OP_TERM_POL_AUTHORIZE.";
		ORDER = 0;
		AUDITABLE = 0;
		ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
		SERIALIZABLE = 0;

		#===================
		#  Fields 
		#===================

		STRING PIN_FLD_NAME {

			DESCR = "Extension name. For RADIUS, this name must match a name from the dictionary. e.g. Framed-IP-Netmask, Framed-MTU.";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 255;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_VALUE {

			DESCR = "Extension value. Stringified version of the value for the corresponding extension name. e.g. for IP addrs this is in dotted decimal notation. For enumerated values, either enumerated name or decimal value may be used (e.g. PPP or 1 for Framed-Protocol)";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 1023;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

	}

	SUBSTRUCT PIN_FLD_SERVICE_IP {

		DESCR = "Substruct to hold IP service specific information. Note that these entries are obsoleted by the PIN_FLD_ARGS array and are supported only for backward compatibility.";
		ORDER = 0;
		AUDITABLE = 0;
		ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
		SERIALIZABLE = 0;

		#===================
		#  Fields 
		#===================

		ENUM PIN_FLD_COMPRESSION {

			DESCR = "Compression option (for protocol). (Framed-Compression)";
			ORDER = 0;
			CREATE = Optional;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		BINSTR PIN_FLD_IPADDR {

			DESCR = "IP addres. Zero length means dynamically assigned.  (Framed-IP-Address)";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 160;
			CREATE = Optional;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		ENUM PIN_FLD_PROTOCOL {

			DESCR = "SLIP, PPP etc. (Framed-Protocol)";
			ORDER = 0;
			CREATE = Optional;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

	}

}

#=======================================

STORABLE CLASS /service/ip IMPLEMENTATION ORACLE7 {


	#===================
	#  Fields 
	#===================

	ARRAY PIN_FLD_ARGS {

		SQL_TABLE = "service_ai_g2_t";
		SQL_STORAGE = "tablespace pin00 storage (initial 200k next 200k maxextents unlimited pctincrease 0 ) ";

		#===================
		#  Fields 
		#===================

		STRING PIN_FLD_NAME {

			SQL_COLUMN = "name";
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_VALUE {

			SQL_COLUMN = "value";
		}

	}

	SUBSTRUCT PIN_FLD_SERVICE_IP {

		SQL_TABLE = "service_ai_g2_t";
		SQL_STORAGE = "tablespace pin00 storage (initial 200k next 200k maxextents unlimited pctincrease 0 ) ";

		#===================
		#  Fields 
		#===================

		ENUM PIN_FLD_COMPRESSION {

			SQL_COLUMN = "compression";
		}

		BINSTR PIN_FLD_IPADDR {

			SQL_COLUMN = "ipaddr";
		}

		ENUM PIN_FLD_PROTOCOL {

			SQL_COLUMN = "protocol";
		}

	}

}

#=======================================
#  Storable Class /service/my_ai5
#  Group 05 Custom AI Service
#=======================================

STORABLE CLASS /service/my_ai5 {

        READ_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";
        WRITE_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";
        DESCR = "Group 05 custom AI service. Stores AI service metadata as name/value pairs.";
        IS_PARTITIONED = "0";
        EVENT_TYPE = "NONE";

        #===================
        #  Fields
        #===================

        ARRAY PIN_FLD_ARGS {

                DESCR = "Group 05 AI service argument array. List of name/value pairs used to store AI service configuration or metadata. Element id is not significant.";
                ORDER = 0;
                AUDITABLE = 0;
                ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
                SERIALIZABLE = 0;

                #===================
                #  Fields
                #===================

                STRING PIN_FLD_NAME {

                        DESCR = "Group 05 AI service argument name.";
                        ORDER = 0;
                        LENGTH = 255;
                        CREATE = Required;
                        MODIFY = Writeable;
                        AUDITABLE = 0;
                        ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
                        SERIALIZABLE = 0;
                }

                STRING PIN_FLD_VALUE {

                        DESCR = "Group 05 AI service argument value.";
                        ORDER = 0;
                        LENGTH = 1023;
                        CREATE = Required;
                        MODIFY = Writeable;
                        AUDITABLE = 0;
                        ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
                        SERIALIZABLE = 0;
                }

        }

}

		}

			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 60;
			CREATE = Optional;

			SERIALIZABLE = 0;

			ORDER = 0;

STORABLE CLASS /service/nextAI_gr04 {

	READ_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";
	WRITE_ACCESS = "BrandLineage";
	DESCR = "Provides dialup Internet access. Includes connection information, such as IP address and connection protocol.";
	IS_PARTITIONED = "0";
	EVENT_TYPE = "NONE";

	#===================
	#  Fields 
	#===================

	ARRAY PIN_FLD_ARGS {

		DESCR = "Terminal Server Protocol Extensions Array. List of (name, value) pairs which are used to set the IP session characteristics. Element id not significant.  MultnextAI_gr04le entries with same name may be used if appropriate. See PCM_OP_TERM_POL_AUTHORIZE.";
		ORDER = 0;
		AUDITABLE = 0;
		ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
		SERIALIZABLE = 0;

		#===================
		#  Fields 
		#===================

		STRING PIN_FLD_NAME {

			DESCR = "Extension name. For RADIUS, this name must match a name from the dictionary. e.g. Framed-IP-Netmask, Framed-MTU.";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 255;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_VALUE {

			DESCR = "Extension value. Stringified version of the value for the corresponding extension name. e.g. for IP addrs this is in dotted decimal notation. For enumerated values, either enumerated name or decimal value may be used (e.g. PPP or 1 for Framed-Protocol)";
			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 1023;
			CREATE = Required;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

	}

}

#!/bin/bash
# Generate customers CSV with random plans.
#
#   bash generate_customers_100.csv.sh              # rows 1-100
#   bash generate_customers_100.csv.sh 101 200      # rows 101-200
#   bash generate_customers_100.csv.sh 1 50         # rows 1-50

set -e

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
# shellcheck source=plans.sh
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/plans.sh"

START="${1:-1}"
END="${2:-100}"
OUTPUT="customers_${START}_${END}.csv"

echo "login,password,first_name,last_name,email,country,zip,state,city,address,billing_name,billing_email,plan_code" > "$OUTPUT"

for i in $(seq "$START" "$END"); do
    num=$(printf "%03d" "$i")
    login="nextaiuser${num}"
    password="$CUSTOMER_PASSWORD"
    first="Test${i}"
    last="Nguyen"
    email="${login}@example.com"
    billing_name="${first} ${last}"
    plan=$(pick_random_plan)

    echo "${login},${password},${first},${last},${email},US,95113,CA,San Jose,${i} Test Lane,${billing_name},${email},${plan}" >> "$OUTPUT"
done

echo "Wrote $((END - START + 1)) customers to ${OUTPUT} (rows ${START}-${END})"

login,password,first_name,last_name,email,country,zip,state,city,address,billing_name,billing_email,plan_code
nextaiuser101,AQ123456789aq!,Test101,Nguyen,nextaiuser101@example.com,US,95113,CA,San Jose,101 Test Lane,Test101 Nguyen,nextaiuser101@example.com,Plan Oddisey 3.5 Token 40USD Group 1
nextaiuser102,AQ123456789aq!,Test102,Nguyen,nextaiuser102@example.com,US,95113,CA,San Jose,102 Test Lane,Test102 Nguyen,nextaiuser102@example.com,Plan Oddisey 3.5 Token PAYG Group 1
nextaiuser103,AQ123456789aq!,Test103,Nguyen,nextaiuser103@example.com,US,95113,CA,San Jose,103 Test Lane,Test103 Nguyen,nextaiuser103@example.com,Plan Oddisey 3.5 Token PAYG Group 1
nextaiuser104,AQ123456789aq!,Test104,Nguyen,nextaiuser104@example.com,US,95113,CA,San Jose,104 Test Lane,Test104 Nguyen,nextaiuser104@example.com,Plan Oddisey 3.5 Token 40USD Group 1
nextaiuser105,AQ123456789aq!,Test105,Nguyen,nextaiuser105@example.com,US,95113,CA,San Jose,105 Test Lane,Test105 Nguyen,nextaiuser105@example.com,Plan Oddisey 3.5 Token 40USD Group 1

nextaiuser107,AQ123456789aq!,Test107,Nguyen,nextaiuser107@example.com,US,95113,CA,San Jose,107 Test Lane,Test107 Nguyen,nextaiuser107@example.com,Plan Oddisey 3 Prompt 10USD Group 1

#!/bin/bash
# Create customers ŌĆö same flist as cust_test.nap, number increments, random plan.
#
#   cd $PIN_HOME/sys/test
#   bash create_100_customers.sh
#   bash create_100_customers.sh --from 30
#   bash create_100_customers.sh --from 30 --to 100

set -e
set +H

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/plans.sh"

START=1
END=100
LOG_DIR="customer_create_logs"
NAP_FILE="./nextaig1/nap/cust_bulk.nap"

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --from) START="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --to)   END="$2";   shift 2 ;;
        *) echo "Usage: $0 [--from N] [--to N]"; exit 1 ;;
    esac
done

mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" nextaig1/nap

ok=0
fail=0

for i in $(seq "$START" "$END"); do
    num=$(printf "%03d" "$i")
    login="nextaiuser${num}"
    plan=$(pick_random_plan)
    out="${LOG_DIR}/${login}.out"

    cat > "$NAP_FILE" <<EOF
r << END 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID          POID [0] \$DB_NO /account 1 0
0 PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ   POID [0] \$DB_NO /account 1 0
0 PIN_FLD_PROGRAM_NAME  STR [0] "testnap"
0 PIN_FLD_PASSWD_CLEAR  STR [0] "Aq123456789aq!"
0 PIN_FLD_LOGIN         STR [0] "${login}"
0 PIN_FLD_CODE          STR [0] "${plan}"
0 PIN_FLD_LAST_NAME     STR [0] "Nguyen"
0 PIN_FLD_FIRST_NAME    STR [0] "Test${i}"
0 PIN_FLD_EMAIL_ADDR    STR [0] "${login}@example.com"
0 PIN_FLD_COUNTRY       STR [0] "US"
0 PIN_FLD_ZIP           STR [0] "95113"
0 PIN_FLD_STATE         STR [0] "CA"
0 PIN_FLD_CITY          STR [0] "San Jose"
0 PIN_FLD_ADDRESS       STR [0] "${i} Test Lane"
0 PIN_FLD_NAME          STR [0] "Test${i} Nguyen"
0 PIN_FLD_DELIVERY_DESCR STR [0] "Invoice"
END
xop 280214 0 1
q
EOF

    echo "[${i}/${END}] ${login} -> ${plan}"
    if testnap "$NAP_FILE" > "$out" 2>&1; then
        if grep -qE 'PIN_FLD_ERROR_CODE\s+STR \[0\] "0"' "$out"; then
            ok=$((ok + 1))
        else
            fail=$((fail + 1))
            echo "  failed (see ${out})"
        fi
    else
        fail=$((fail + 1))
        echo "  testnap error (see ${out})"
    fi
done

echo "Done. success=${ok} failed=${fail} (logs in ${LOG_DIR}/)"

#!/bin/bash

OUT_FILE="usage_rate_t2_101_correct_account.nap"
ACCOUNT_POID="851719"
LOGIN="nait2test042_t2_allowance_prompt"
DATE_STR="06-19-2026"
OPCODE="205502"
RUN_ID=$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)

rm -f "$OUT_FILE"

for i in $(seq -w 1 101)
do
cat >> "$OUT_FILE" <<EON
r << xx 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account ${ACCOUNT_POID} 0
0 PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ    POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account ${ACCOUNT_POID} 0
0 PIN_FLD_LOGIN          STR  [0] "${LOGIN}"
0 PIN_FLD_NAME           STR  [0] "${DATE_STR}"
0 PIN_FLD_DESCR          STR  [0] "Odyssey 3.0 monthly fee with included - prompt usage ${i}"
0 PIN_FLD_PROGRAM_NAME   STR  [0] "testnap"
0 PIN_FLD_TRANS_ID_T2    STR  [0] "ODYSSEY3-851719-${RUN_ID}-PROMPT-${i}"
0 PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE_T2  STR  [0] "odyssey-3.0"
0 PIN_FLD_PROMPT_T2      STR  [0] "Prompt usage test number ${i} for Odyssey 3.0 account 851719."
0 PIN_FLD_IN_TOKENS_T2   INT  [0] 0
0 PIN_FLD_OUT_TOKENS_T2  INT  [0] 0
xx

xop ${OPCODE} 0 1

EON
done

echo "Created $OUT_FILE with 101 usage events for account ${ACCOUNT_POID}, login ${LOGIN}, run ${RUN_ID}."

#=======================================
#  Field PIN_FLD_INFO_NAI3
#=======================================

SUBSTRUCT PIN_FLD_INFO_NAI3 {

	ID = 10012;
}


#=======================================
#  Field PIN_FLD_INPUT_TOKENS_NAI3
#=======================================

INT PIN_FLD_INPUT_TOKENS_NAI3 {

	ID = 10013;
}


#=======================================
#  Field PIN_FLD_MODELTYPE_NAI3
#=======================================

ENUM PIN_FLD_MODELTYPE_NAI3 {

	ID = 10009;
	DESCR = "Model type tracks which Odyssey model (3.0 vs 3.5) per service (group3)";
}


#=======================================
#  Field PIN_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS_NAI3
#=======================================

INT PIN_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS_NAI3 {

	ID = 10014;
}


#=======================================
#  Field PIN_FLD_PROMPT_NAI3
#=======================================

STRING PIN_FLD_PROMPT_NAI3 {

	ID = 10015;
}


#=======================================
#  Field PIN_FLD_TRANSACTION_ID
#=======================================

DECIMAL PIN_FLD_TRANSACTION_ID {

	ID = 8102;
}


#=======================================
#  Storable Class /event/session/usageg3
#=======================================

STORABLE CLASS /event/session/usageg3 {

	READ_ACCESS = "Self";
	WRITE_ACCESS = "Self";
	IS_PARTITIONED = "1";
	PARTITION_MODE = "Finite";
	EVENT_TYPE = "USAGE_PREPAID";

	#===================
	#  Fields 
	#===================

	SUBSTRUCT PIN_FLD_INFO_NAI3 {

		ORDER = 0;
		AUDITABLE = 0;
		ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
		SERIALIZABLE = 0;

		#===================
		#  Fields 
		#===================

		INT PIN_FLD_INPUT_TOKENS_NAI3 {

			ORDER = 0;
			CREATE = Optional;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		ENUM PIN_FLD_MODELTYPE_NAI3 {

			DESCR = "Model type tracks which Odyssey model (3.0 vs 3.5) per service (group3)";
			ORDER = 0;
			CREATE = Optional;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		INT PIN_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS_NAI3 {

			ORDER = 0;
			CREATE = Optional;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_PROMPT_NAI3 {

			ORDER = 0;
			LENGTH = 60;
			CREATE = Optional;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

		DECIMAL PIN_FLD_TRANSACTION_ID {

			ORDER = 0;
			CREATE = Optional;
			MODIFY = Writeable;
			AUDITABLE = 0;
			ENCRYPTABLE = 0;
			SERIALIZABLE = 0;
		}

	}

}


#=======================================
#  Storable Class /event/session/usageg3
#=======================================

STORABLE CLASS /event/session/usageg3 IMPLEMENTATION ORACLE7 {


	#===================
	#  Fields 
	#===================

	SUBSTRUCT PIN_FLD_INFO_NAI3 {

		SQL_TABLE = "usage_info_nai3";

		#===================
		#  Fields 
		#===================

		INT PIN_FLD_INPUT_TOKENS_NAI3 {

			SQL_COLUMN = "input_tokens";
		}

		ENUM PIN_FLD_MODELTYPE_NAI3 {

			SQL_COLUMN = "model";
		}

		INT PIN_FLD_OUTPUT_TOKENS_NAI3 {

			SQL_COLUMN = "output_tokens";
		}

		STRING PIN_FLD_PROMPT_NAI3 {

			SQL_COLUMN = "prompt";
		}

		DECIMAL PIN_FLD_TRANSACTION_ID {

			SQL_COLUMN = "transaction";
		}

	}

}

#!/bin/bash

for i in $(seq 1 5)
do
  cp usage_rate_prompt_t2.nap tmp_usage_$i.nap
  sed -i "s/ODYSSEY3-T2-PROMPT-0002/ODYSSEY3-T2-PROMPT-$i/g" tmp_usage_$i.nap

  echo "Running usage $i"
  testnap tmp_usage_$i.nap
done
r << xx 1

xop 205502 0 1

#************************************************************************
# Configuration file for the testnap utility
#
#
# You use testnap to manually interact with the database and to test your
# custom applications.
#
# This configuration file is automatically installed and configured with
# default values during Portal installation. You can edit this file to:
#   -- change the default values of the entries.
#   -- disable an entry by inserting a crosshatch (#) at the start of
#        the line.
#   -- enable a commented entry by removing the crosshatch (#).
# 
# Before you make any changes to this file, save a backup copy.
#
# When editing this file, follow the instructions in each section.
# For more information on the general syntax of configuration entries,
# see "Reference Guide to Portal Configuration Files" in the Portal
# online documentation.
#************************************************************************


#======================================================================
#
# You can edit this file to suit your specific configuration:
#  -- You can change the default values of an entry.
#  -- You can exclude an optional entry by adding the # symbol
#     at the beginning of the line.
#  -- You can include a commented entry by removing the # symbol.
#
# Before you make any changes to this file, save a backup copy.
#
# To edit this file, follow the instructions in the commented sections.
# For more information on the general syntax of configuration entries,
# see "Reference Guide to Portal Configuration Files" in the Portal
# online documentation.
#======================================================================

#=======================================================================
# ptr_virtual_time
# 
# Enables the use of pin_virtual_time to advance Portal time, and 
# specifies the shared file for use by all Portal mangers.
#
# The entry has 2 values
#
# #/Empty     	to disable / enable pin_virtual_time in all pin.conf files
#		default = #	(disable)
#
# <filepath> 	location and name of the pin_virtual_time file shared by all Portal managers.
#		default = ${PIN_HOME}/lib/pin_virtual_time_file
#
#=======================================================================
- - pin_virtual_time ${PIN_HOME}/lib/pin_virtual_time_file



#========================================================================
# cm_ptr
# 
# Specifies a pointer to the CM or CMMP. 
#
# Use a separate entry for each CM or CMMP. If testnap can't find the
# first CM or CMMP, it looks for the next in the list.
#
# Each entry includes three values:
# 
#     <protocol> = "ip", for this release
#     <host>     = the name or IP address of the computer running the
#                    CM or CMMP
#     <port>     = the TCP port number of the CM or CMMP on this computer
#
# The port number should match a corresponding cm_ports entry with
# the same port number in the CM or CMMP configuration file. The 
# default, 11960, is a commonly specified port for the CM or CMMP.
#========================================================================
- nap cm_ptr ip congero-brm-training 11960


#========================================================================
# logfile
#
# Specifies the path to the log file for the sample application
#========================================================================
- sample_act    logfile sample_act.pinlog
- sample_del    logfile sample_del.pinlog
- sample_app    logfile sample_app.pinlog 
- sample_search logfile sample_search.pinlog 
- sample_who    logfile sample_who.pinlog


#========================================================================
# login_type
#
# Specifies whether the login name and password are required.
#
# The value for this entry can be:
#
#    0 = Only a user ID is required.
#    1 = (Default) Both a name and a password are required.
#
# By default, CMs require a user login and password when requesting an open
# context using PCM_CONTEXT_OPEN. However, you can remove this authentication
# requirement by configuring the CM with a cm_login_module of
# cm_login_null.so
#========================================================================
- nap login_type 1


#========================================================================
# login_name
#
# Specifies the login name to use when testnap connects to the CM. 
#========================================================================
- nap login_name root.0.0.0.1


#========================================================================
# login_pw
#
# Specifies the password to use when testnap connects to the CM.
#========================================================================
#- nap login_pw   


#========================================================================
# userid
#
# Specifies the database number and service type for the Portal
# database. 
#
# The CM uses the database number to identify the Portal database 
# and to connect to the correct Data Manager. For connections that don't
# require a login name and password, the CM also passes the service
# type to the database.
#
# The database number, in the form 0.0.0.N, is the number assigned to 
# your Portal database when you installed the system. The default
# is 0.0.0.1.
#
# The service type is /service/pcm_client and the ID is set based on login_name.
# DO NOT change these values.
#========================================================================
- - userid 0.0.0.1 /service/pcm_client 1


#========================================================================
# enable_ssl
#
# Specifies whether TLS will be enabled for PCM connections
#
#    0 - TLS is disabled.
#    1 - TLS is enabled.
#
#========================================================================
- nap enable_ssl 0

# Group 3 Custom Field
#- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/g3_fields.dat

# Define .dat file
- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/nextai_fields.dat

# Custom fields/opcodes dat file
- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/combined.dat

# Load custom FM group 1
- cm fm_module /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/lib/fm_nai_custom.so fm_nai_config - pin


#========================================================================
# Group 4 add extension file
- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/include/ops/naig4_cust_ops.dat
- - ops_fields_extension_file /opt/app/brm/pin/BRM/bin/naig4_acc_cust_ops.dat
#========================================================================
r << xx 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account 851719 0
0 PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ    POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account 851719 0
0 PIN_FLD_LOGIN          STR  [0] "nait2test042"
0 PIN_FLD_NAME           STR  [0] "06-19-2026"
0 PIN_FLD_DESCR          STR  [0] "Team 2 OP_RATE test usage"
0 PIN_FLD_PROGRAM_NAME   STR  [0] "testnap"
0 PIN_FLD_TRANS_ID_T2    STR  [0] "T2-TEST-0001"
0 PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE_T2  STR  [0] "Odissey 3.0"
0 PIN_FLD_PROMPT_T2      STR  [0] "Hello, this is a Team 2 usage rating test."
0 PIN_FLD_IN_TOKENS_T2   INT  [0] 25
0 PIN_FLD_OUT_TOKENS_T2  INT  [0] 50
xx

xop 205502 0 1
#!/bin/bash

OUT_FILE="usage_rate_t2_101_869667.nap"
ACCOUNT_POID="869667"
LOGIN="new_nait2test042_t2_prompt"
DATE_STR="10-12-2026"
OPCODE="205502"
RUN_ID=$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)

rm -f "$OUT_FILE"

for i in $(seq -w 1 101)
do
cat >> "$OUT_FILE" <<EON
r << xx 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account ${ACCOUNT_POID} 0
0 PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ    POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account ${ACCOUNT_POID} 0
0 PIN_FLD_LOGIN          STR  [0] "${LOGIN}"
0 PIN_FLD_NAME           STR  [0] "${DATE_STR}"
0 PIN_FLD_DESCR          STR  [0] "Odyssey 3.0 T2 prompt usage ${i}"
0 PIN_FLD_PROGRAM_NAME   STR  [0] "testnap"
0 PIN_FLD_TRANS_ID_T2    STR  [0] "ODYSSEY3-869667-${RUN_ID}-PROMPT-${i}"
0 PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE_T2  STR  [0] "odyssey-3.0"
0 PIN_FLD_PROMPT_T2      STR  [0] "Prompt usage test number ${i} for Odyssey 3.0 account 869667."
0 PIN_FLD_IN_TOKENS_T2   INT  [0] 0
0 PIN_FLD_OUT_TOKENS_T2  INT  [0] 0
xx

xop ${OPCODE} 0 1

EON
done

echo "Created $OUT_FILE with 101 usage events for account ${ACCOUNT_POID}, login ${LOGIN}, run ${RUN_ID}."
r << xx 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account 869667 0
0 PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ    POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account 869667 0
0 PIN_FLD_LOGIN          STR  [0] "new_nait2test042_t2_prompt"
0 PIN_FLD_NAME           STR  [0] "10-12-2026"
0 PIN_FLD_DESCR          STR  [0] "Odyssey 3.0 T2 prompt usage 001"
0 PIN_FLD_PROGRAM_NAME   STR  [0] "testnap"
0 PIN_FLD_TRANS_ID_T2    STR  [0] "ODYSSEY3-869667-20260619145150-PROMPT-001"
0 PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE_T2  STR  [0] "odyssey-3.0"
0 PIN_FLD_PROMPT_T2      STR  [0] "Prompt usage test number 001 for Odyssey 3.0 account 869667."
0 PIN_FLD_IN_TOKENS_T2   INT  [0] 0
0 PIN_FLD_OUT_TOKENS_T2  INT  [0] 0
xx

xop 205502 0 1

r << xx 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account 869667 0
0 PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ    POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account 869667 0
0 PIN_FLD_LOGIN          STR  [0] "new_nait2test042_t2_prompt"
0 PIN_FLD_NAME           STR  [0] "10-12-2026"

#!/bin/bash

OUT_FILE="usage_rate_t2_101.nap"
ACCOUNT_POID="851719"
LOGIN="nait2test042"
DATE_STR="06-19-2026"
OPCODE="205502"

rm -f "$OUT_FILE"

for i in $(seq -w 1 101)
do
cat >> "$OUT_FILE" <<EON
r << xx 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account ${ACCOUNT_POID} 0
0 PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ    POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account ${ACCOUNT_POID} 0
0 PIN_FLD_LOGIN          STR  [0] "${LOGIN}"
0 PIN_FLD_NAME           STR  [0] "${DATE_STR}"
0 PIN_FLD_DESCR          STR  [0] "Odyssey 3.0 monthly fee with included - prompt usage ${i}"
0 PIN_FLD_PROGRAM_NAME   STR  [0] "testnap"
0 PIN_FLD_TRANS_ID_T2    STR  [0] "ODYSSEY3-T2-PROMPT-${i}"
0 PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE_T2  STR  [0] "odyssey-3.0"
0 PIN_FLD_PROMPT_T2      STR  [0] "Prompt usage test number ${i} for Odyssey 3.0."
0 PIN_FLD_IN_TOKENS_T2   INT  [0] 0
0 PIN_FLD_OUT_TOKENS_T2  INT  [0] 0
xx

xop ${OPCODE} 0 1

EON
done

echo "Created $OUT_FILE with 101 usage events."
r << xx 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account 851719 0
0 PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ    POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account 851719 0
0 PIN_FLD_LOGIN          STR  [0] "nait2test042"
0 PIN_FLD_NAME           STR  [0] "06-19-2026"
0 PIN_FLD_DESCR          STR  [0] "Odyssey 3.0 monthly fee with included - prompt usage 001"
0 PIN_FLD_PROGRAM_NAME   STR  [0] "testnap"
0 PIN_FLD_TRANS_ID_T2    STR  [0] "ODYSSEY3-T2-PROMPT-001"
0 PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE_T2  STR  [0] "odyssey-3.0"
0 PIN_FLD_PROMPT_T2      STR  [0] "Prompt usage test number 001 for Odyssey 3.0."
0 PIN_FLD_IN_TOKENS_T2   INT  [0] 0
0 PIN_FLD_OUT_TOKENS_T2  INT  [0] 0
xx

xop 205502 0 1

r << xx 1
0 PIN_FLD_POID           POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account 851719 0
0 PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ    POID [0] 0.0.0.1 /account 851719 0
0 PIN_FLD_LOGIN          STR  [0] "nait2test042"
0 PIN_FLD_NAME           STR  [0] "06-19-2026"
0 PIN_FLD_DESCR          STR  [0] "Odyssey 3.0 monthly fee with included - prompt usage 002"
0 PIN_FLD_PROGRAM_NAME   STR  [0] "testnap"
0 PIN_FLD_TRANS_ID_T2    STR  [0] "ODYSSEY3-T2-PROMPT-002"
0 PIN_FLD_MODEL_CODE_T2  STR  [0] "odyssey-3.0"
0 PIN_FLD_PROMPT_T2      STR  [0] "Prompt usage test number 002 for Odyssey 3.0."
0 PIN_FLD_IN_TOKENS_T2   INT  [0] 0
0 PIN_FLD_OUT_TOKENS_T2  INT  [0] 0
xx

xop 205502 0 1
